General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.

      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.

Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML) using a CLI tool like the 'file' command to check the actual file format. When corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed — do not call Read on this file. Instead: (1) When images are from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) When the retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.

Reproducible testing.
   - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. When you cannot reproduce the problem, you cannot verify the fix.
   - When encountering logic bugs, write an automated test that fails due to the bug, then implement the fix to make it pass.
   - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.
   - When creating tests, prefer minimum reproducible examples - the simplest test case that demonstrates the issue.

   - When fixing UI bugs, capture both the \"before\" (problem) and \"after\" (fixed) screenshots as evidence for human verification.

        "content": "{\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nReplace `/home/sandbox` with `/workspace` as the canonical HOME and working directory across all Docker images, decoupling user identity from filesystem layout.\\n\\n### Problem\\nThe current setup tightly couples user identity (`sandbox`) with filesystem paths (`/home/sandbox`). Downstream images like hive-mind are forced to rename users, move home directories, and recursively `chown` thousands of files — slow, fragile, and unnecessary.\\n\\n### Solution\\n\\n- **`/workspace`** is now the shared, user-agnostic HOME directory\\n- The `sandbox` user is created with `-d /workspace` (GID/UID managed by group)\\n- ACL (`setfacl`) enforces group-writable permissions on `/workspace`\\n- All toolchain paths (`pyenv`, `cargo`, `sdkman`, `nvm`, `rbenv`, etc.) moved from `/home/sandbox/.<tool>` to `/workspace/.<tool>`\\n- `ENV HOME=/workspace` set explicitly in final images\\n- `chmod 2775 /workspace` ensures setgid bit for group-shared access\\n\\n### Files changed (27 files)\\n\\n| Category | Files |\\n|----------|-------|\\n| Base image | `ubuntu/24.04/js/Dockerfile` — user creation with `-d /workspace`, group setup |\\n| Essentials | `ubuntu/24.04/essentials-sandbox/Dockerfile` — ACL install + setfacl |\\n| Essentials script | `ubuntu/24.04/essentials-sandbox/install.sh` — sandbox user `-d /workspace` |\\n| Language images | `python`, `go`, `rust`, `java`, `kotlin`, `ruby`, `php`, `perl`, `swift`, `lean`, `rocq` Dockerfiles |\\n| Standalone images | `assembly`, `cpp`, `dotnet`, `r` Dockerfiles |\\n| Assembly images | `ubuntu/24.04/full-sandbox/Dockerfile`, root `Dockerfile` |\\n| Scripts | `ubuntu/24.04/common.sh`, `scripts/measure-disk-space.sh`, `scripts/ubuntu-24-server-install.sh` |\\n| CI | `.github/workflows/release.yml` |\\n| Docs | `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `README.md` |\\n| Version | `.changeset/workspace-home-migration.md` (minor bump → 1.5.0) |\\n\\n### Downstream usage (no hacks needed)\\n\\n```dockerfile\\nRUN useradd -m -d /workspace -s /bin/bash hive && \\\\\\n    usermod -aG sandbox hive\\n\\nUSER hive\\nENV HOME=/workspace\\nWORKDIR /workspace\\n```\\n\\nNo more `mv /home/sandbox /home/hive`, no more `chown -R`.\\n\\n### Verification checklist\\n\\n- [x] All Provide a summary of the changes\n37\t\n38\tThe changeset will be saved as a markdown file in `.changeset/` and should be committed with your PR.\n39\t\n40\t#### Changeset Guidelines\n41\t\n42\t- **Patch**: Bug fixes, documentation updates, internal refactoring\n43\t- **Minor**: New features, non-breaking enhancements\n44\t- **Major**: Breaking changes that affect the public API\n45\t\n46\tExample changeset summary:\n47\t\n48\t```markdown\n49\tAdd support for automatic fork creation with --auto-fork flag\n50\t```\n51\t\n52\t#### Release Process\n53\t\n54\t1. When PRs with changesets are merged to main, the Release workflow automatically creates a \"Version Packages\" PR\n55\t2. The Version Packages PR updates package.json versions and CHANGELOG.md\n56\t3. When the Version Packages PR is merged, the package is automatically published to NPM\n57\t\n58\t### AI Agent Configuration\n59\t\n60\t```typescript\n61\tinterface AgentConfig {\n62\t  model: 'sonnet' | 'haiku' | 'opus';\n63\t  priority: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'critical';\n64\t  specialization?: string[];\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\texport const defaultConfig: AgentConfig = {\n68\t  model: 'sonnet',\n69\t  priority: 'medium',\n70\t  specialization: ['code-review', 'issue-solving'],\n71\t};\n72\t```\n73\t\n74\t### Quality Gates\n75\t\n76\tBefore merging, ensure:\n77\t\n78\t- [ ] All tests pass\n79\t- [ ] File size limits enforced\n80\t- [ ] Type checking passes\n81\t- [ ] Human review completed\n82\t- [ ] AI consensus achieved (if multi-agent)\n83\t\n84\t### Communication Protocols\n85\t\n86\t#### Human → AI\n87\t\n88\t```bash\n89\t# Clear, specific instructions\n90\t./solve.mjs https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --requirements \"Security focus, maintain backward compatibility\"\n91\t```\n92\t\n93\t#### AI → Human\n94\t\n95\t```bash\n96\t# Status reports with actionable items\n97\techo \"🤖 Analysis complete. Requires human decision on breaking changes.\"\n98\t```\n99\t\n100\t## Testing AI Agents\n101\t\n102\t```typescript\n103\timport { testAgent } from './tests/agent-testing.ts';\n104\t\n105\t// Test agent behavior\n106\tawait testAgent({\n107\t  scenario: 'complex-issue-solving',\n108\t  expectedOutcome: 'pull-request-created',\n109\t  timeout: 300000, // 5 minutes\n110\t});\n111\t```\n112\t\n113\t## Code Review Process\n114\t\n115\t1. **Automated Review** - AI agents perform initial analysis\n116\t2. **Cross-Agent Validation** - Multiple agents verify solutions\n117\t3. **Human Oversight** - Final architectural and security review\n118\t4. **Consensus Building** - Resolve conflicts through discussion\n119\t\n120\t### Review Checklist\n121\t\n122\t- [ ] Algorithm correctness verified\n123\t- [ ] Security vulnerabilities assessed\n124\t- [ ] Performance implications considered\n125\t- [ ] Documentation completeness\n126\t- [ ] Integration test coverage\n127\t"

This approach is kept for reference only.\n\n> **Note:** As of issue #1394, the `ubuntu-24-server-install.sh` script has been removed from the repository.\n> The Docker image now uses `konard/sandbox` (pinned to a specific version) as the base image, which provides all development tools.\n> For historical reference, the last version of the script is available at:\n> https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/4f027b32/scripts/ubuntu-24-server-install.sh\n\n## Steps\n\n1. Reset/install VPS/VDS server with fresh Ubuntu 24.04\n2. Login to `root` user.\n3. Install sandbox first (provides all development tools)\n\n   ```bash\n   # Option 1: Use Docker (recommended)\n   docker pull konard/sandbox:1.3.16\n   docker run -it konard/sandbox:1.3.16\n\n   # Option 2: Use the sandbox install script (pinned to v1.3.16 release commit)\n   curl -fsSL -o- https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/raw/178aa3816ab2c2150844fb967ffa329c63b90131/ubuntu/24.04/full-sandbox/install.sh | bash\n   ```\n\n   **Note:** The installation does NOT run `gh auth login` automatically. This is intentional to support Docker builds without timeouts. Authentication is performed in the next steps.\n\n4. Login to `hive` user\n\n   ```bash\n   su - hive\n   ```\n\n5. **IMPORTANT:** Authenticate with GitHub CLI AFTER installation is complete\n\n   ```bash\n   gh-setup-git-identity\n   ```\n\n   Note: Follow the prompts to authenticate with your GitHub account. This is required for the gh tool to work, and the system will perform all actions using this GitHub account. This step must be done AFTER the installation script completes to avoid build timeouts in Docker environments.\n\n6. Claude Code CLI, OpenCode AI CLI, and @link-assistant/agent are preinstalled with the previous script. Now you need to make sure claude is authorized. Execute claude command, and follow all steps to authorize the local claude\n\n   ```bash\n   claude\n   ```\n\n   Note: Both opencode and agent come with free Grok Code Fast 1 model by default - so no authorization is required for these tools.\n\n7. Launch the Hive Mind telegram bot:\n\n   **Using Links Notation (recommended):**\n\n   ```\n   screen -R bot # Enter new screen for bot\n\n   hive-telegram-bot --configuration \"\n     TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN: '849...355:AAG...rgk_YZk...aPU'\n     TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHATS:\n       -1002975819706\n       -1002861722681\n     TELEGRAM_HIVE_OVERRIDES:\n       --all-issues\n       --once\n       --skip-issues-with-prs\n       --attach-logs\n       --verbose\n       --no-tool-check\n     TELEGRAM_SOLVE_OVERRIDES:\n       --attach-logs\n       --verbose\n       --no-tool-check\n     TELEGRAM_BOT_VERBOSE: true\n   \"\n\n   # Press CTRL + A + D for detach from screen\n   ```\n\n   **Using individual command-line options:**\n\n   ```\n   screen -R bot # Enter new screen for bot\n\n   hive-telegram-bot --token 849...355:AAG...rgk_YZk...aPU --allowed-chats \"(\n     -1002975819706\n     -1002861722681\n   )\" --hive-overrides \"(\n     --all-issues\n     --once\n     --skip-issues-with-prs\n     --attach-logs\n     --verbose\n     --no-tool-check\n   )\" --solve-overrides \"(\n     --attach-logs\n     --verbose\n     --no-tool-check\n   )\" --verbose\n\n   # Press CTRL + A + D for detach from screen\n   ```\n\n   Note: You may need to register you own bot with https://t.me/BotFather to get the bot token.\n\n## Codex sign-in\n\n1. Connect to your instance of VPS with Hive Mind installed, using SSH with tunnel opened\n\n```bash\nssh -L 1455:localhost:1455 root@123.123.123.123\n```\n\n2. Start codex login oAuth server:\n\n```bash\ncodex login\n```\n\nThe oAuth callback server on 1455 port will be started, and the link to oAuth will be printed, copy the link.\n\n3. Use your browser on machine where you started the tunnel from, paste there the link from `codex login` command, and go there using your browser. Once redirected to localhost:1455 you will see successful login page, and in `codex login` you will see `Successfully logged in`. After that `codex login` command will complete, and you can use `codex` command as usual to verify. It should also be working with `--tool codex` in `solve` and `hive` commands.\n",

[2026-03-30T08:07:50.612Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
    "id": "msg_01ShDf6FJFHqpVUmx2NhajrV",
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_01N8TapStWQnac4AREUWbvVA",
        "name": "Write",
        "input": {
          "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1774857876871/docs/case-studies/issue-1499/README.md",
          "content": "# Case Study: Issue #1499 — Migrate to Sandbox 1.5.0 `/workspace` Layout\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nThe hive-mind Docker image inherits from `konard/sandbox:1.3.16` and renames the\n`sandbox` user to `hive`, moves the home directory from `/home/sandbox` to\n`/home/hive`, and recursively `chown`s thousands of files. This approach is:\n\n1. **Slow** — `chown -R` and `usermod -d ... -m` traverse every installed\n   runtime (pyenv, cargo, sdkman, nvm, rbenv, etc.), adding minutes to each\n   build.\n2. **Fragile** — hardcoded paths in init scripts (`nvm.sh`, `.bashrc`, opam\n   config) must be patched with `sed`, which is error-prone and breaks when\n   upstream adds new files.\n3. **Source of permission bugs** — issues #1419 (root-owned `.config`) and\n   #1394 (sandbox→hive migration) stem directly from the rename approach.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\nThe coupling between user identity (`sandbox`) and filesystem layout\n(`/home/sandbox`) forced every downstream image to choose between:\n\n- **Rename** — expensive, fragile, and error-prone (current hive-mind approach)\n- **Symlink** — creates confusion between real and symlinked paths\n- **Run as sandbox** — loses the ability to have a distinct user identity\n\n## Solution: Sandbox 1.5.0 `/workspace` Architecture\n\nSandbox PR [sandbox#73](https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/pull/73)\n(merged as v1.5.0) decouples user identity from filesystem layout:\n\n| Before (≤1.3.x)                       | After (1.5.0)                        |\n|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|\n| `sandbox` user, HOME=`/home/sandbox`   | `sandbox` user, HOME=`/workspace`    |\n| Downstream must rename user & paths    | Downstream adds user to `sandbox` group |\n| `chown -R` on thousands of files       | Zero `chown` needed                  |\n| `sed` patches for hardcoded paths      | No path fixups needed                |\n| Permission issues from rename          | ACL-based group permissions          |\n\n### How it works\n\n1. Sandbox creates a `sandbox` group and user with `-d /workspace`\n2. All runtimes installed under `/workspace/.*` (`.pyenv`, `.nvm`, `.cargo`, etc.)\n3. `setfacl` grants the `sandbox` group rwx access to `/workspace`\n4. `chmod 2775` sets the setgid bit so new files inherit group ownership\n\n### Downstream usage (hive-mind)\n\n```dockerfile\nFROM konard/sandbox:1.5.0\nUSER root\nRUN useradd -m -d /workspace -s /bin/bash -g sandbox hive\nUSER hive\nENV HOME=/workspace\nWORKDIR /workspace\n```\n\nNo rename, no chown, no sed patches. The `hive` user is in the `sandbox` group\nand has full access to all runtimes in `/workspace`.\n\n## Changes Made\n\n### Dockerfiles (root + coolify)\n- Bumped `FROM konard/sandbox:1.3.16` → `FROM konard/sandbox:1.5.0`\n- Replaced 30+ lines of user rename / chown / sed with 1-line `useradd`\n- Changed all `/home/hive` ENV vars to `/workspace`\n- Changed `WORKDIR` from `/home/hive` to `/workspace`\n\n### Scripts\n- `coolify/start.sh`: All `/home/hive` paths → `/workspace`\n- `docker-restore-auth.sh`: Already used `/workspace` paths (no change needed)\n- `scripts/verify-docker-image.sh`: Updated user/path checks\n\n### Configuration\n- `coolify/docker-compose.yml`: Volume mounts updated to `/workspace`\n\n### Documentation\n- `docs/UBUNTU-SERVER.md`: Updated sandbox version reference\n\n### CI/CD\n- `.github/workflows/release.yml`: Removed `usermod` failure check (no longer applicable)\n\n## Impact\n\n- **Build time**: Eliminates expensive `chown -R` and `usermod -d -m` operations\n- **Reliability**: No more `sed`-based path fixups that can silently fail\n- **Maintainability**: Upstream sandbox changes to runtimes work transparently\n- **Security**: ACL-based permissions are more explicit than ownership-based access\n\n## References\n\n- Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1499\n- Sandbox PR: https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/pull/73\n- Sandbox release: v1.5.0\n- Related issues: #1394, #1419\n"
        },
        "caller": {
          "type": "direct"
        }
      }
    ],
    "stop_reason": null,

    "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1774857876871/docs/case-studies/issue-1499/README.md",
    "content": "# Case Study: Issue #1499 — Migrate to Sandbox 1.5.0 `/workspace` Layout\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nThe hive-mind Docker image inherits from `konard/sandbox:1.3.16` and renames the\n`sandbox` user to `hive`, moves the home directory from `/home/sandbox` to\n`/home/hive`, and recursively `chown`s thousands of files. This approach is:\n\n1. **Slow** — `chown -R` and `usermod -d ... -m` traverse every installed\n   runtime (pyenv, cargo, sdkman, nvm, rbenv, etc.), adding minutes to each\n   build.\n2. **Fragile** — hardcoded paths in init scripts (`nvm.sh`, `.bashrc`, opam\n   config) must be patched with `sed`, which is error-prone and breaks when\n   upstream adds new files.\n3. **Source of permission bugs** — issues #1419 (root-owned `.config`) and\n   #1394 (sandbox→hive migration) stem directly from the rename approach.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\n\nThe coupling between user identity (`sandbox`) and filesystem layout\n(`/home/sandbox`) forced every downstream image to choose between:\n\n- **Rename** — expensive, fragile, and error-prone (current hive-mind approach)\n- **Symlink** — creates confusion between real and symlinked paths\n- **Run as sandbox** — loses the ability to have a distinct user identity\n\n## Solution: Sandbox 1.5.0 `/workspace` Architecture\n\nSandbox PR [sandbox#73](https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/pull/73)\n(merged as v1.5.0) decouples user identity from filesystem layout:\n\n| Before (≤1.3.x)                       | After (1.5.0)                        |\n|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|\n| `sandbox` user, HOME=`/home/sandbox`   | `sandbox` user, HOME=`/workspace`    |\n| Downstream must rename user & paths    | Downstream adds user to `sandbox` group |\n| `chown -R` on thousands of files       | Zero `chown` needed                  |\n| `sed` patches for hardcoded paths      | No path fixups needed                |\n| Permission issues from rename          | ACL-based group permissions          |\n\n### How it works\n\n1. Sandbox creates a `sandbox` group and user with `-d /workspace`\n2. All runtimes installed under `/workspace/.*` (`.pyenv`, `.nvm`, `.cargo`, etc.)\n3. `setfacl` grants the `sandbox` group rwx access to `/workspace`\n4. `chmod 2775` sets the setgid bit so new files inherit group ownership\n\n### Downstream usage (hive-mind)\n\n```dockerfile\nFROM konard/sandbox:1.5.0\nUSER root\nRUN useradd -m -d /workspace -s /bin/bash -g sandbox hive\nUSER hive\nENV HOME=/workspace\nWORKDIR /workspace\n```\n\nNo rename, no chown, no sed patches. The `hive` user is in the `sandbox` group\nand has full access to all runtimes in `/workspace`.\n\n## Changes Made\n\n### Dockerfiles (root + coolify)\n- Bumped `FROM konard/sandbox:1.3.16` → `FROM konard/sandbox:1.5.0`\n- Replaced 30+ lines of user rename / chown / sed with 1-line `useradd`\n- Changed all `/home/hive` ENV vars to `/workspace`\n- Changed `WORKDIR` from `/home/hive` to `/workspace`\n\n### Scripts\n- `coolify/start.sh`: All `/home/hive` paths → `/workspace`\n- `docker-restore-auth.sh`: Already used `/workspace` paths (no change needed)\n- `scripts/verify-docker-image.sh`: Updated user/path checks\n\n### Configuration\n- `coolify/docker-compose.yml`: Volume mounts updated to `/workspace`\n\n### Documentation\n- `docs/UBUNTU-SERVER.md`: Updated sandbox version reference\n\n### CI/CD\n- `.github/workflows/release.yml`: Removed `usermod` failure check (no longer applicable)\n\n## Impact\n\n- **Build time**: Eliminates expensive `chown -R` and `usermod -d -m` operations\n- **Reliability**: No more `sed`-based path fixups that can silently fail\n- **Maintainability**: Upstream sandbox changes to runtimes work transparently\n- **Security**: ACL-based permissions are more explicit than ownership-based access\n\n## References\n\n- Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1499\n- Sandbox PR: https://github.com/link-foundation/sandbox/pull/73\n- Sandbox release: v1.5.0\n- Related issues: #1394, #1419\n",
    "structuredPatch": [],
    "originalFile": null
  }
}

Installing via apt makes opam accessible system-wide.\nRUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y opam && apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*\n\n# Fix any references to old user in config /home/hive/.nvm/nvm.sh; \\\n    fi && \\\n    if [ -f /home/hive/.nvm/bash_completion ]; then \\\n      sed -i 's|/home/sandbox|/home/hive|g' /home/hive/.nvm/bash_completion; \\\n    fi && \\\n    # install chromium chrome firefox webkit msedge chromium-headless-shell; \\\n    else \\\n      playwright install chromium firefox webkit chromium-headless-shell; \\\n    fi\n\n# Install Playwright OS Installing via apt makes opam accessible system-wide.\nRUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y opam && apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*\n\n# --- Environment variables ---\n# Set environment variables EARLY so they're available in subsequent RUN commands\n# All paths use /workspace (shared directory from sandbox 1.5.0)\nENV HOME=/workspace\nENV NVM_DIR=\"/workspace/.nvm\"\nENV PYENV_ROOT=\"/workspace/.pyenv\"\nENV BUN_INSTALL=\"/workspace/.bun\"\nENV DENO_INSTALL=\"/workspace/.deno\"\nENV CARGO_HOME=\"/workspace/.cargo\"\nENV GOROOT=\"/workspace/.go\"\nENV GOPATH=\"/workspace/.go/path\"\nENV SDKMAN_DIR=\"/workspace/.sdkman\"\nENV PERLBREW_ROOT=\"/workspace/.perl5\"\nENV RBENV_ROOT=\"/workspace/.rbenv\"\n\nENV rocq install script)\nENV PATH=\"/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/opt/php@8.3/bin:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/opt/php@8.3/sbin:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:/workspace/.pyenv/bin:/workspace/.pyenv/shims:/workspace/.rbenv/bin:/workspace/.rbenv/shims:/workspace/.swift/usr/bin:/workspace/.elan/bin:/workspace/.opam/default/bin:/workspace/.local/bin:/workspace/.cargo/bin:/workspace/.deno/bin:/workspace/.bun/bin:/workspace/.go/bin:/workspace/.go/path/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin\"\n\n# \"$NODE_VERSION_DIR/bin\" ]; then \\\n      ln -sf \"$NODE_VERSION_DIR/bin\" /workspace/.node-bin && \\\n      chown -h hive:sandbox /workspace/.node-bin; \\\n    fi\n\nENV PATH=\"/workspace/.node-bin:${PATH}\"\n\n# Switch to hive user for package installations\nUSER hive\nWORKDIR /workspace\n\n# --- AI-specific packages installation ---\n# Install AI coding assistant @qwen-code/qwen-code || echo \"qwen-code: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @google/gemini-cli || echo \"gemini-cli: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @github/copilot || echo \"copilot: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g opencode-ai || echo \"opencode-ai: not yet published\"\n\n# Install hive-mind workflow utilities\nRUN bun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind || echo \"hive-mind: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @link-assistant/claude-profiles || echo \"claude-profiles: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @link-assistant/agent || echo \"agent: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g start-command || echo \"start-command: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g gh-setup-git-identity || echo \"gh-setup-git-identity: install -g gh-load-pull-request || echo \"gh-load-pull-request: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g gh-upload-log || echo \"gh-upload-log: not yet published\"\n\n# --- Playwright Browser Automation Setup ---\n# Note: npm is available via the .node-bin symlink in PATH\nRUN npm install -g @playwright/mcp@latest --no-fund --silent\n\n# Install Playwright CLI and browsers (architecture-aware)\nRUN npm install -g @playwright/test@latest --no-fund --silent && \\\n    ARCH=$(uname -m) && \\\n    if [ \"$ARCH\" = \"x86_64\" ] || [ \"$ARCH\" = \"amd64\" ]; then \\\n Playwright OS dependencies (requires root)\n# Note: HOME is overridden to /root to prevent root processes from creating\n# files under /workspace/.config with root ownership (see issue #1419)\nUSER Application Setup ---\nWORKDIR /app\n\nUSER root\n\n# Copy application files from repo root\nCOPY --chown=hive:sandbox . .\n\n# Ensure scripts are executable\nRUN chmod +x *.mjs 2>/dev/null || true && \\\n    chmod +x src/*.mjs 2>/dev/null || true\n\n# Create necessary directories\nRUN mkdir -p /app/claude-logs /app/claude-sessions /app/output && \\\n    mkdir -p /workspace/.claude/plugins /workspace/.config && \\\n    chown -R hive:sandbox /app /workspace/.claude /workspace/.config\n\n# Create bash profile for hive user\n# Note: .node-bin is a symlink to the active Node.js version's bin directory\nRUN echo 'export PATH=\"/workspace/.node-bin:/workspace/.bun/bin:/workspace/.cargo/bin:/workspace/.elan/bin:/workspace/.opam/default/bin:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH\"' >> /workspace/.bashrc && \\\n    echo 'export BUN_INSTALL=\"/workspace/.bun\"' >> /workspace/.bashrc && \\\n    chown hive:sandbox /workspace/.bashrc\n\n# Create git config file for hive user\nRUN echo \"[user]\" > /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    echo \"  name = Hive-Mind Bot\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    echo \"  email = hive@localhost\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    echo \"[init]\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    echo \"  defaultBranch = main\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    echo \"[safe]\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    echo \"  directory = *\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig && \\\n    chown hive:sandbox /workspace/.gitconfig\n\n# Switch back to hive user for runtime\nUSER hive\n\n# Runtime configuration\nENV NODE_ENV=production\nENV PORT=3000\nENV MONITOR_TAG=\"help wanted\"\nENV CONCURRENCY=2\nENV INTERVAL=300\nENV MODEL=sonnet\n\n# Health check\nHEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=30s --retries=3 \\\n    CMD pgrep -f \"hive.mjs\\|tail\" > /dev/null || exit 1\n\n# Copy and configure startup script\nUSER root\nCOPY --chown=root:root coolify/start.sh /app/start.sh\nRUN chmod +x /app/start.sh\n\nSHELL [\"/bin/bash\",           "content": "#!/bin/bash\necho \"========================================\"\necho \"Hive-Mind Container\"\necho \"========================================\"\n\n# Fix permissions and git config issues (runs as root)\necho \"Fixing permissions and git configuration...\"\n\n# Remove .gitconfig if it's a directory and create proper git config\nif [ -d /workspace/.gitconfig ]; then\n  echo \"  - Removing invalid .gitconfig directory\"\n  rm -rf /workspace/.gitconfig\nfi\n\n# Create proper git config file if it doesn't exist\nif [ ! -f /workspace/.gitconfig ]; then\n  echo \"  - Creating git configuration\"\n  echo \"[user]\" > /workspace/.gitconfig\n  echo \"  name = Hive-Mind Bot\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig\n  echo \"  email = hive@localhost\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig\n  echo \"[init]\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig\n  echo \"  defaultBranch = main\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig\n  echo \"[safe]\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig\n  echo \"  directory = *\" >> /workspace/.gitconfig\n  chown hive:sandbox /workspace/.gitconfig\nfi\n\n# Fix Claude and GitHub config directories\necho \"  - Setting up Claude and GitHub directories\"\nmkdir -p /workspace/.claude/plugins /workspace/.config/gh\nchown -R hive:sandbox /workspace/.claude /workspace/.config\nchown -R hive:sandbox /app/claude-logs /app/claude-sessions /app/output 2>/dev/null || true\n\n# Ensure /tmp has proper permissions for git operations\nchmod 1777 /tmp\n\n# Pass environment to hive user and run main logic\nexec su -s /bin/bash hive -c '\ncd /app\n\n# Set token if provided\nif [ -n \"$GITHUB_TOKEN\" ]; then\n  export GH_TOKEN=\"$GITHUB_TOKEN\"\nelif [ -n \"$GH_TOKEN\" ]; then\n  export GITHUB_TOKEN=\"$GH_TOKEN\"\nfi\n\n# Set PATH for installed tools\nexport PATH=\"/workspace/.bun/bin:/workspace/.node-bin:/workspace/.cargo/bin:$PATH\"\n\n# Check if we have auth and URL\nif gh auth status >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -n \"$GITHUB_URL\" ]; then\n  echo \"✓ GitHub authenticated\"\n\n  # Build hive-mind command with all environment variables as CLI options\n  HIVE_CMD=\"node hive.mjs \\\"$GITHUB_URL\\\"\"\n\n  # Add options based on environment variables\n  [ -n \"$MONITOR_TAG\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --monitor-tag \\\"$MONITOR_TAG\\\"\"\n  [ \"$ALL_ISSUES\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --all-issues\"\n  [ \"$SKIP_ISSUES_WITH_PRS\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --skip-issues-with-prs\"\n  [ -n \"$CONCURRENCY\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --concurrency $CONCURRENCY\"\n  [ -n \"$PULL_REQUESTS_PER_ISSUE\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --pull-requests-per-issue $PULL_REQUESTS_PER_ISSUE\"\n  [ -n \"$MODEL\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --model $MODEL\"\n  [ -n \"$INTERVAL\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --interval $INTERVAL\"\n  [ -n \"$MAX_ISSUES\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --max-issues $MAX_ISSUES\"\n  [ \"$DRY_RUN\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --dry-run\"\n  [ \"$VERBOSE\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --verbose\"\n  [ \"$ONCE\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --once\"\n  [ -n \"$MIN_DISK_SPACE\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --min-disk-space $MIN_DISK_SPACE\"\n  [ \"$AUTO_CLEANUP\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --auto-cleanup\"\n  [ \"$FORK\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --fork\"\n  [ \"$ATTACH_LOGS\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --attach-logs\"\n  [ -n \"$PROJECT_NUMBER\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --project-number $PROJECT_NUMBER\"\n  [ -n \"$PROJECT_OWNER\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --project-owner \\\"$PROJECT_OWNER\\\"\"\n  [ -n \"$PROJECT_STATUS\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --project-status \\\"$PROJECT_STATUS\\\"\"\n  [ \"$PROJECT_MODE\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --project-mode\"\n\n  # YouTrack integration options\n  [ \"$YOUTRACK_MODE\" = \"true\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --youtrack-mode\"\n  [ -n \"$YOUTRACK_PROJECT_CODE\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --youtrack-project \\\"$YOUTRACK_PROJECT_CODE\\\"\"\n  [ -n \"$YOUTRACK_STAGE\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --youtrack-stage \\\"$YOUTRACK_STAGE\\\"\"\n\n  # Target branch for PRs\n  [ -n \"$TARGET_BRANCH\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD --target-branch \\\"$TARGET_BRANCH\\\"\"\n\n  # Add any additional arguments from HIVE_ARGS\n  [ -n \"$HIVE_ARGS\" ] && HIVE_CMD=\"$HIVE_CMD $HIVE_ARGS\"\n\n  # Try to start hive-mind, but catch failures\n  echo \"✓ Starting hive-mind to monitor: $GITHUB_URL\"\n  echo \"  Command: $HIVE_CMD\"\n  eval $HIVE_CMD\n  EXIT_CODE=$?\n\n  if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then\n    echo \"\"\n    echo \"⚠ Hive-mind failed to start (exit code: $EXIT_CODE)\"\n    echo \"\"\n    echo \"Common issues:\"\n    echo \"  - Claude CLI not authenticated: Run '\\''claude'\\'' or '\\''code'\\'' to authenticate\"\n    echo \"  - Missing API key: Set CLAUDE_API_KEY environment variable\"\n    echo \"\"\n    echo \"Container running. Access terminal to configure.\"\n    echo \"Keeping container alive...\"\n    # Keep container running without consuming CPU\n    tail -f /dev/null\n  fi\nelse\n  echo \"\"\n  if ! gh auth status >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n    echo \"⚠ GitHub not authenticated. Run: gh auth login\"\n  fi\n  if [ -z \"$GITHUB_URL\" ]; then\n    echo \"⚠ GITHUB_URL not set. Set it in Coolify environment variables\"\n  fi\n  echo \"\"\n  echo \"Container running. Access terminal to configure.\"\n  echo \"Keeping container alive...\"\n  # Keep container running without consuming CPU\n  tail -f /dev/null\nfi\n'\n"

then\n  echo \"hive user is in sandbox group: OK\"\nelse\n  echo \"ERROR: hive user is not in the sandbox group\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\n# Verify .config directory ownership (see issue #1419)\n# Root-owned .config prevents tools from creating config subdirectories at runtime\nif [ -d /workspace/.config ]; then\n    echo \"ERROR: /workspace/.config is owned by $CONFIG_OWNER, expected hive or sandbox\"\n    echo \"This causes EACCES errors when tools try to create config subdirectories\"\n    echo \"See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1419\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  echo \".config directory ownership: OK\"\nelse\n  echo \".config directory does not exist yet (will be created at runtime): OK\"\nfi\n\n# Verify hive user can create directories in .config (see issue #1419)\nif mkdir -p /workspace/.config/.verify-test 2>/dev/null; then\n  rmdir /workspace/.config/.verify-test 2>/dev/null\n  echo \".config directory write access: OK\"\nelse\n  echo \"ERROR: hive user cannot create directories in /workspace/.config\"\n  echo \"See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1419\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"User setup verification: PASSED\"\necho \"\"\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 2: Verify all system & development tools (alphabetical order)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\necho \"=== Verifying system & development tools (from sandbox base) ===\"\necho \"Checking all tools in alphabetical order to reduce merge conflicts\"\n\ncheck_tool \"Bun\"       bun       --version\ncheck_tool \"Cargo\"     cargo     --version\ncheck_tool \"Clang\"     clang     --version\ncheck_tool \"Clang++\"   clang++   --version\ncheck_tool \"CMake\"     cmake     --version\ncheck_tool \"Deno\"      deno      --version\ncheck_tool \"Elan\"      elan      --version\ncheck_tool \"G++\"       g++       --version\ncheck_tool \"GCC\"       gcc       --version\ncheck_tool \"Git\"       git       --version\ncheck_tool \"GitHub CLI\" gh       --version\ncheck_tool \"Go\"        go        version\ncheck_tool \"Homebrew\"  brew      --version\ncheck_tool \"Java\"      java      -version\ncheck_tool \"Lake\"      lake      --version\ncheck_tool \"Lean\"      lean      --version\ncheck_tool \"LLD Linker\" lld      --version\ncheck_tool \"LLVM\"      llvm-config --version\n\ncheck_tool \"Make\"      make      --version\n\ncheck_tool \"Node.js\"   node      --version\ncheck_tool \"NPM\"       npm       --version\ncheck_tool \"Opam\"      opam      --version\n\necho \"\"\necho \"Checking PHP...\"\nif command -v php &>/dev/null; do\n  BROWSER_DIR=$(ls -d \"${PLAYWRIGHT_CACHE}/${browser}\"* 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)\n  if [ -n \"$BROWSER_DIR\" ] && [ -d \"$BROWSER_DIR\" ]; then\n    echo \"  $browser: OK ($(basename \"$BROWSER_DIR\"))\"\n  else\n    echo \"  $browser: MISSING\"\n    BROWSERS_MISSING=\"$BROWSERS_MISSING $browser\"\n  fi\ndone\n\n# Check optional browsers (chromium_headless_shell, ffmpeg)\nfor browser in chromium_headless_shell ffmpeg; do\n  BROWSER_DIR=$(ls -d \"${PLAYWRIGHT_CACHE}/${browser}\"* 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)\n  if [ -n \"$BROWSER_DIR\" ] && [ -d \"$BROWSER_DIR\" ]; then\n    echo \"  $browser: OK ($(basename \"$BROWSER_DIR\"))\"\n  else\n    echo \"  $browser: not installed (optional)\"\n  fi\ndone\n\nif [ -n \"$BROWSERS_MISSING\" ]; then\n  echo \"ERROR: Required Playwright browsers missing:$BROWSERS_MISSING\"\n  echo \"The Playwright MCP server requires these browsers to function properly.\"\n  echo \"See issue #1060 for more details.\"\n  exit 1\nelse\n  echo \"All required Playwright browsers are installed\"\nfi\n\ncheck_tool \"Python\"    python    --version\ncheck_tool \"Pyenv\"     pyenv     --version\n\necho \"\"\necho \"Checking Rocq/Coq...\"\n# Source opam environment for Rocq/Coq access\n# Reference: https://rocq-prover.org/docs/using-opam\nset +u  # opam init scripts may reference unset variables\nif [ -f \"$HOME/.opam/opam-init/init.sh\" ];     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# verify-docker-image.sh\n#\n# Verifies the hive-mind Docker image has all required tools.\n# Run this script inside the Docker container:\n#\n#   docker run --rm IMAGE bash scripts/verify-docker-image.sh\n#\n# This script verifies:\n#   1. User rename (sandbox -> hive)\n#   2. All system & development tools (from sandbox base image, alphabetical order)\n#   3. AI-specific tools (added by hive-mind on top of sandbox)\n#\n# Exit code 0 = all checks passed; non-zero = one or more checks failed.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Source shell initialisation files so that user-installed tools are on PATH\n# Third-party init scripts may reference unset variables, so we temporarily\n# disable the unbound-variable check (set -u) while sourcing them.\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\nexport HOME=/home/hive\n# Add ~/.local/bin for user-installed binaries (e.g. opam installed by rocq install script)\nexport PATH=\"$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH\"\n\n# Disable -u temporarily for all third-party init scripts\nset +u\n\n[ -s \"$HOME/.nvm/nvm.sh\" ]           && source \"$HOME/.nvm/nvm.sh\"\n[ -s \"$HOME/.cargo/env\" ]            && source \"$HOME/.cargo/env\"\n[ -s \"$HOME/.elan/env\" ]             && source \"$HOME/.elan/env\"\n\nexport PYENV_ROOT=\"$HOME/.pyenv\"\nexport PATH=\"$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH\"\nif command -v pyenv &>/dev/null; then\n  eval \"$(pyenv init --path)\"\n  eval \"$(pyenv init -)\"\nfi\n\nexport SDKMAN_DIR=\"$HOME/.sdkman\"\nif [ -s \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\" ]; then\n  source \"$SDKMAN_DIR/bin/sdkman-init.sh\"\nfi\n\n# Source Go environment (GOPATH moved to .go/path in issue #1004)\nif [ -d \"$HOME/.go\" ]; then\n  export GOROOT=\"$HOME/.go\"\n  export GOPATH=\"$HOME/.go/path\"\n  export PATH=\"$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH\"\nfi\n\n# Perlbrew moved to .perl5 in issue #1004\nexport PERLBREW_ROOT=\"$HOME/.perl5\"\n[ -s \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\" ] && source \"$PERLBREW_ROOT/etc/bashrc\"\n\n# Re-enable strict mode for our own code\nset -u\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Helper: check a single command\n# Usage: check_tool \"Display Name\" command [--version-flag]\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\ncheck_tool() {\n  local name=\"$1\"\n  local cmd=\"$2\"\n  local ver_flag=\"${3:---version}\"\n  echo \"\"\n  echo \"Checking ${name}...\"\n  if command -v \"$cmd\" &>/dev/null; then\n    \"$cmd\" $ver_flag 2>&1 | head -n1 || true\n    echo \"${name} is accessible\"\n  else\n    echo \"${name} command not found in container\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n}\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 1: Verify user rename (sandbox -> hive)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\necho \"=== Verifying user rename (sandbox -> hive) ===\"\necho \"\"\n\nCURRENT_USER=$(whoami)\necho \"Current user: $CURRENT_USER\"\nif [ \"$CURRENT_USER\" != \"hive\" ]; then\n  echo \"ERROR: Expected user hive, got $CURRENT_USER\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\nif [ \"$HOME\" != \"/home/hive\" ]; then\n  echo \"ERROR: HOME should be /home/hive, got $HOME\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\nif [ ! -d /home/hive ]; then\n  echo \"ERROR: /home/hive directory does not exist\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\nif [ ! -w /home/hive ]; then\n  echo \"ERROR: /home/hive is not writable by hive user\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\n# Verify .config directory ownership (see issue #1419)\n# Root-owned .config prevents tools from creating config subdirectories at runtime\nif [ -d /home/hive/.config ]; then\n  CONFIG_OWNER=$(stat -c '%U' /home/hive/.config 2>/dev/null || stat -f '%Su' /home/hive/.config 2>/dev/null)\n  echo \".config directory owner: $CONFIG_OWNER\"\n  if [ \"$CONFIG_OWNER\" != \"hive\" ]; then\n    echo \"ERROR: /home/hive/.config is owned by $CONFIG_OWNER, expected hive\"\n    echo \"This causes EACCES errors when tools try to create config subdirectories\"\n    echo \"See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1419\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  echo \".config directory ownership: OK\"\nelse\n  echo \".config directory does not exist yet (will be created at runtime): OK\"\nfi\n\n# Verify hive user can create directories in .config (see issue #1419)\nif mkdir -p /home/hive/.config/.verify-test 2>/dev/null; then\n  rmdir /home/hive/.config/.verify-test 2>/dev/null\n  echo \".config directory write access: OK\"\nelse\n  echo \"ERROR: hive user cannot create directories in /home/hive/.config\"\n  echo \"See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1419\"\n  exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"User rename verification: PASSED\"\necho \"\"\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step 2: Verify all system & development tools (alphabetical order)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\necho \"=== Verifying system & development tools (from sandbox base) ===\"\necho \"Checking all tools in alphabetical order to reduce merge conflicts\"\n\ncheck_tool \"Bun\"       bun       --version\ncheck_tool \"Cargo\"     cargo     --version\ncheck_tool \"Clang\"     clang     --version\ncheck_tool \"Clang++\"   clang++   --version\ncheck_tool \"CMake\"     cmake     --version\ncheck_tool \"Deno\"      deno      --version\ncheck_tool \"Elan\"      elan      --version\ncheck_tool \"G++\"       g++       --version\ncheck_tool \"GCC\"       gcc       --version\ncheck_tool \"Git\"       git       --version\ncheck_tool \"GitHub CLI\" gh       --version\ncheck_tool \"Go\"        go        version\ncheck_tool \"Homebrew\"  brew      --version\ncheck_tool \"Java\"      java      -version\ncheck_tool \"Lake\"      lake      --version\ncheck_tool \"Lean\"      lean      --version\ncheck_tool \"LLD Linker\" lld      --version\ncheck_tool \"LLVM\"      llvm-config --version\n\ncheck_tool \"Make\"      make      --version\n\ncheck_tool \"Node.js\"   node      --version\ncheck_tool \"NPM\"       npm       --version\ncheck_tool \"Opam\"      opam      --version\n\necho \"\"\necho \"Checking PHP...\"\nif command -v php &>/dev/null; Connect to your instance of VPS with Hive Mind installed, using SSH with tunnel opened\n\n```bash\nssh -L 1455:localhost:1455 root@123.123.123.123\n```\n\n2. Use your browser on machine where you started the tunnel from, paste there the link from `codex login` command, and go there using your browser. Once redirected to localhost:1455 you will see successful login page, and in `codex login` you will see `Successfully logged in`. After that `codex login` command will complete, and you can use `codex` command as usual to verify. It should also be working with `--tool codex` in `solve` and `hive` commands.\n",

uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Use Node.js 20.x\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: 24.x\n\n      - name: Validate documentation files\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running documentation validation tests...\"\n          chmod +x tests/docs-validation.mjs\n          node tests/docs-validation.mjs\n\n  # === DOCKER PR CHECK ===\n  docker-pr-check:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check, test-compilation, lint, check-file-line-limits]\n    # Run if: changeset-check succeeded OR was skipped (docs-only PR), and required checks passed\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      !cancelled() &&\n      !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') &&\n      github.event_name == 'pull_request' &&\n      (needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.test-compilation.result == 'success' || needs.test-compilation.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.check-file-line-limits.result == 'success' || needs.check-file-line-limits.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.detect-changes.outputs.docker-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    env:\n      REGISTRY: docker.io\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Free up disk space\n        run: node scripts/free-disk-space.mjs\n\n      - name: Show sandbox base image version\n        run: |\n          SANDBOX_VERSION=$(grep '^FROM konard/sandbox:' Dockerfile | sed 's/FROM konard\\/sandbox://')\n          echo \"Sandbox base image version: konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION}\"\n          echo \"This pinned version ensures stable, reproducible builds.\"\n\n      - name: Build Docker image\n        run: |\n          SANDBOX_VERSION=$(grep '^FROM konard/sandbox:' Dockerfile | sed 's/FROM konard\\/sandbox://')\n          echo \"Building Docker image from konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION} base...\"\n          echo \"Note: General-purpose tools are inherited from pinned konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION}\"\n          echo \"This image adds AI-specific tools on top of the sandbox base.\"\n          echo \"\"\n\n          # Note: Multi-platform builds (amd64+arm64) are tested in docker-publish job during release\n          # PR checks only validate amd64 using standard docker build (not buildx) to ensure\n          # the image is properly loaded into Docker daemon for container verification\n          docker build --progress=plain -t ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:test . 2>&1 | tee build-output.log\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"Docker image built successfully\"\n          docker images | grep -E \"${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}|REPOSITORY\"\n\n      - name: Verify build completed without errors\n        run: |\n          echo \"Checking build logs for critical errors...\"\n\n          # Check specifically for usermod failure (sandbox -> hive rename)\n          if grep -E 'usermod: command not found|usermod: user.*not found' build-output.log; then\n            echo \"ERROR: User rename from sandbox to hive failed\"\n            exit 1\n          fi\n\n          # Check for unbound variable errors (indicates shell script issues with set -u compatibility)\n          if grep -E 'unbound variable' build-output.log; then\n            echo \"ERROR: Unbound variable error detected in Docker build\"\n            echo \"This typically indicates a shell script issue with set -u compatibility\"\n            grep -E 'unbound variable' build-output.log || true\n            exit 1\n          fi\n\n          echo \"Build log check completed\"\n\n      - name: Verify system & development tools in running container\n        run: |\n          SANDBOX_VERSION=$(grep '^FROM konard/sandbox:' Dockerfile | sed 's/FROM konard\\/sandbox://')\n          echo \"=== Verifying hive-mind Docker image ===\"\n          echo \"Base: konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION} (pinned) + AI-specific tools\"\n          echo \"\"\n\n          # Run verification script inside container (mounts script as read-only volume)\n          # Verifies: user rename (sandbox->hive), all dev tools, AI-specific tools\n          # See scripts/verify-docker-image.sh for the full list of checks\n          docker run --rm \\\n            -v \"$(pwd)/scripts/verify-docker-image.sh:/verify-docker-image.sh:ro\" \\\n            ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:test \\\n            bash /verify-docker-image.sh\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"All system & development tools verification checks passed!\"\n\n  # === HELM PR CHECK (FAST) ===\n  helm-pr-check:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check]\n    # Only runs when helm chart, package.json, or workflow files changed (Issue #1436)\n    if: always() && github.event_name == 'pull_request' && (needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') && (needs.detect-changes.outputs.helm-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Install Helm\n        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4\n        with:\n          version: v3.14.0\n\n      - name: Get package version\n        id: package-version\n        run: |\n          VERSION=$(node -p \"require('./package.json').version\")\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Package version: $VERSION\"\n\n      - name: Lint Helm chart\n        run: |\n          echo \"Linting Helm chart...\"\n          helm lint helm/hive-mind\n          echo \"Helm chart validation passed\"\n\n      - name: Verify Chart.yaml structure\n        run: bash scripts/verify-chart-yaml.sh\n\n  # === RELEASE - only runs on main after tests pass ===\n  release:\n    name: Release\n    needs: [detect-changes, lint, test-suites, test-execution, memory-check-linux]\n    if: always() && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push' && !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') && (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') && (needs.test-suites.result == 'success' || needs.test-suites.result == 'skipped') && (needs.test-execution.result == 'success' || needs.test-execution.result == 'skipped') && (needs.memory-check-linux.result == 'success' || needs.memory-check-linux.result == 'skipped') && (needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.docker-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    outputs:\n      published: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published }}\n      published_version: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\n    # Permissions required for npm OIDC trusted publishing\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      pull-requests: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: '24.x'\n          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm install\n\n      - name: Update npm for OIDC trusted publishing\n        run: node scripts/setup-npm.mjs\n\n      - name: Check for changesets\n        id: check_changesets\n        run: |\n          # Count changeset files (excluding README.md and config.json)\n          CHANGESET_COUNT=$(find .changeset -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" | wc -l)\n          echo \"Found $CHANGESET_COUNT changeset file(s)\"\n          echo \"has_changesets=$([[ $CHANGESET_COUNT -gt 0 ]] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"changeset_count=$CHANGESET_COUNT\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Harmonize changeset bump types\n        if: steps.check_changesets.outputs.has_changesets == 'true' && steps.check_changesets.outputs.changeset_count > 1\n        run: |\n          echo \"Multiple changesets detected, harmonizing bump types...\"\n          node scripts/merge-changesets.mjs\n\n      - name: Version packages and commit to main\n        if: steps.check_changesets.outputs.has_changesets == 'true'\n        id: version\n        run: node scripts/version-and-commit.mjs --mode changeset\n\n      - name: Publish to npm\n        # Run if version was committed OR if a previous attempt already committed (for re-runs)\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        id: publish_npm\n        run: node scripts/publish-to-npm.mjs --should-pull\n\n      - name: Set publish outputs\n        # This step explicitly sets the outputs using shell (more reliable than Node.js appendFileSync)\n        # It runs after npm publish and reads the script's outputs to pass them along\n        id: publish\n        if: steps.publish_npm.outputs.published == 'true' || steps.publish_npm.outputs.already_published == 'true'\n        run: |\n          echo \"published=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"published_version=${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Docker publish will be triggered with version: ${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/create-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n      - name: Format GitHub release notes\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/format-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\" --commit-sha \"${{ github.sha }}\"\n\n      - name: Post-publish - Upload Source Maps to Sentry\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"Uploading source maps to Sentry for published packages...\"\n          chmod +x scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs\n          node scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs\n\n  # === DOCKER PUBLISH ===\n  # Multi-platform builds using native runners (no QEMU). See: docs/case-studies/issue-982/\n  # ARM64 may be slow: actions/runner-images#11790, actions/partner-runner-images#101\n  # See also: docs/case-studies/issue-998/README.md\n  docker-publish:\n    name: Docker Publish (${{ matrix.platform }})\n    needs: [release]\n    # !cancelled() allows concurrency cancellation. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    # Timeout 60 min: arm64 can take 26+ min. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n    timeout-minutes: 60\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include:\n          - platform: linux/amd64\n            runner: ubuntu-latest\n            cache_suffix: amd64\n          - platform: linux/arm64\n            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm # Native ARM64, no QEMU. See: docs/case-studies/issue-998/\n            cache_suffix: arm64\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      REGISTRY: docker.io\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    outputs:\n      digest-amd64: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\n      digest-arm64: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Free up disk space\n        run: node scripts/free-disk-space.mjs\n\n      - name: Wait for NPM package availability\n        # Only run on first matrix job to avoid duplicate waits\n        if: matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64'\n        run: node scripts/wait-for-npm.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Build and push by digest\n        id: build\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v7\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ./Dockerfile\n          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}\n          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}\n          # Registry cache for faster parallel export. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }}\n          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }},mode=max,image-manifest=true,ignore-error=true\n          outputs: type=image,name=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }},push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true\n\n      - name: Export digest\n        run: |\n          mkdir -p /tmp/digests\n          digest=\"${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\"\n          touch \"/tmp/digests/${digest#sha256:}\"\n          echo \"Digest for ${{ matrix.platform }}: $digest\"\n\n      - name: Upload digest\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7\n        with:\n          name: digests-${{ matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64' && 'amd64' || 'arm64' }}\n          path: /tmp/digests/*\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          retention-days: 1\n\n  # Merge multi-platform manifests after all platform builds complete\n  docker-publish-merge:\n    name: Docker Publish (Merge)\n    needs: [release, docker-publish]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Download digests\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8\n        with:\n          path: /tmp/digests\n          pattern: digests-*\n          merge-multiple: true\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n            type=raw,value=latest\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Create manifest list and push\n        working-directory: /tmp/digests\n        run: |\n          echo \"Creating multi-platform manifest...\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n          echo \"Digests:\"\n          ls -la\n          docker buildx imagetools create $(jq -cr '.tags | map(\"-t \" + .) | join(\" \")' <<< \"$DOCKER_METADATA_OUTPUT_JSON\") \\\n            $(printf '${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@sha256:%s ' *)\n\n      - name: Inspect image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Inspecting multi-platform image...\"\n          docker buildx imagetools inspect ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Verify published image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Docker image published successfully\"\n          echo \"Image: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n\n  # === HELM CHART RELEASE ===\n  helm-release:\n    name: Helm Release\n    needs: [release, docker-publish-merge]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish-merge.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      packages: write\n      pages: write\n      id-token: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Install Helm\n        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4\n        with:\n          version: v3.14.0\n\n      - name: Release Helm chart\n        run: node scripts/helm-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Upload chart to release\n        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2\n        with:\n          tag_name: v${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n          files: .helm-packages/*.tgz\n\n  # === MANUAL INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  instant-release:\n    name: Instant Release\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.release_mode == 'instant'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    outputs:\n      published: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published }}\n      published_version: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      pull-requests: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: '24.x'\n          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm install\n\n      - name: Update npm for OIDC trusted publishing\n        run: node scripts/setup-npm.mjs\n\n      - name: Version packages and commit to main\n        id: version\n        run: node scripts/version-and-commit.mjs --mode instant --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n\n      - name: Publish to npm\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        id: publish_npm\n        run: node scripts/publish-to-npm.mjs\n\n      - name: Set publish outputs\n        # This step explicitly sets the outputs using shell (more reliable than Node.js appendFileSync)\n        id: publish\n        if: steps.publish_npm.outputs.published == 'true' || steps.publish_npm.outputs.already_published == 'true'\n        run: |\n          echo \"published=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"published_version=${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Docker publish will be triggered with version: ${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/create-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n      - name: Format GitHub release notes\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/format-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\" --commit-sha \"${{ github.sha }}\"\n\n  # === DOCKER PUBLISH FOR INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  # Same multi-platform setup as docker-publish above (native runners, no QEMU).\n  # See: docs/case-studies/issue-982/README.md and docs/case-studies/issue-998/README.md\n  docker-publish-instant:\n    name: Docker Publish Instant (${{ matrix.platform }})\n    needs: [instant-release]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    # Timeout 60 min: arm64 can take 26+ min. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n    timeout-minutes: 60\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include:\n          - platform: linux/amd64\n            runner: ubuntu-latest\n            cache_suffix: amd64\n          - platform: linux/arm64\n            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm # Native ARM64, no QEMU. See: docs/case-studies/issue-998/\n            cache_suffix: arm64\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      REGISTRY: docker.io\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Free up disk space\n        run: node scripts/free-disk-space.mjs\n\n      - name: Wait for NPM package availability\n        # Only run on first matrix job to avoid duplicate waits\n        if: matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64'\n        run: node scripts/wait-for-npm.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Build and push by digest\n        id: build\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v7\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ./Dockerfile\n          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}\n          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}\n          # Registry cache for faster parallel export. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }}\n          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }},mode=max,image-manifest=true,ignore-error=true\n          outputs: type=image,name=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }},push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true\n\n      - name: Export digest\n        run: |\n          mkdir -p /tmp/digests\n          digest=\"${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\"\n          touch \"/tmp/digests/${digest#sha256:}\"\n          echo \"Digest for ${{ matrix.platform }}: $digest\"\n\n      - name: Upload digest\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7\n        with:\n          name: digests-instant-${{ matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64' && 'amd64' || 'arm64' }}\n          path: /tmp/digests/*\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          retention-days: 1\n\n  # Merge multi-platform manifests after all platform builds complete (instant release)\n  docker-publish-instant-merge:\n    name: Docker Publish Instant (Merge)\n    needs: [instant-release, docker-publish-instant]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish-instant.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Download digests\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8\n        with:\n          path: /tmp/digests\n          pattern: digests-instant-*\n          merge-multiple: true\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n            type=raw,value=latest\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Create manifest list and push\n        working-directory: /tmp/digests\n        run: |\n          echo \"Creating multi-platform manifest...\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n          echo \"Digests:\"\n          ls -la\n          docker buildx imagetools create $(jq -cr '.tags | map(\"-t \" + .) | join(\" \")' <<< \"$DOCKER_METADATA_OUTPUT_JSON\") \\\n            $(printf '${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@sha256:%s ' *)\n\n      - name: Inspect image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Inspecting multi-platform image...\"\n          docker buildx imagetools inspect ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Verify published image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Docker image published successfully\"\n          echo \"Image: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n\n  # === HELM CHART RELEASE FOR INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  helm-release-instant:\n    name: Helm Release (Instant)\n    needs: [instant-release, docker-publish-instant-merge]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish-instant-merge.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      packages: write\n      pages: write\n      id-token: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Install Helm\n        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4\n        with:\n          version: v3.14.0\n\n      - name: Release Helm chart\n        run: node scripts/helm-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Upload chart to release\n        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2\n        with:\n          tag_name: v${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n          files: .helm-packages/*.tgz\n\n  # === MANUAL CHANGESET PR ===\n  changeset-pr:\n    name: Create Changeset PR\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.release_mode == 'changeset-pr'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      pull-requests: write\n    steps:\n      - only runs on PRs with code changes ===\n  # Docs-only PRs (./docs folder, markdown files) don't require changesets\n  changeset-check:\n    name: Check for Changesets\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: '24.x'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm install\n\n      - name: Check for changesets\n        env:\n          # Pass PR context to the validation script\n          GITHUB_BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}\n          GITHUB_BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\n          GITHUB_HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\n        run: |\n          # Skip changeset check for automated version PRs\n          if [[ \"${{ github.head_ref }}\" == \"changeset-release/\"* ]]; then\n            echo \"Skipping changeset check for automated release PR\"\n            exit 0\n          fi\n\n          # Run changeset validation script\n          # This validates that exactly ONE changeset was ADDED by this PR\n          # Pre-existing changesets from other merged PRs are ignored\n          node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs\n\n  # === FAST CHECKS (FAIL FAST) ===\n  # These checks run quickly (~7-21 seconds each) and should pass before long-running tests\n\n  # === COMPILATION & SYNTAX CHECKS ===\n  test-compilation:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check]\n    # Run if: push event, OR changeset-check succeeded, OR changeset-check was skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: always() && (github.event_name == 'push' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') && (needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: 24.x\n\n      - name: Test solve.mjs compilation\n        run: |\n          echo \"Testing solve.mjs compilation...\"\n          timeout 30s node --check src/solve.mjs\n          echo \"solve.mjs compiles successfully\"\n\n      - name: Test hive.mjs compilation\n        run: |\n          echo \"Testing hive.mjs compilation...\"\n          timeout 30s node --check src/hive.mjs\n          echo \"hive.mjs compiles successfully\"\n\n      - name: Check Node.js syntax for all .mjs files\n        run: bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh\n\n  # === ESLINT CODE QUALITY CHECK ===\n  # Lint runs independently of changeset-check - it's a fast check that should always run\n  # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1023 for why this dependency was removed\n  # IMPORTANT: ESLint now includes max-lines rule (1500 lines) to synchronize with check-file-line-limits\n  # See docs/case-studies/issue-1141 for why fresh merge simulation is critical\n  lint:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.mjs-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          # For PRs, fetch enough history to merge with base branch\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)\n        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'\n        env:\n          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}\n        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: 24.x\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm ci\n\n      - name: Run Prettier format check\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running Prettier format check...\"\n          npm run format:check\n          echo \"Prettier format check passed\"\n\n      - name: Run ESLint\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running ESLint code quality checks...\"\n          npm run lint\n          echo \"ESLint checks passed\"\n\n      - name: Run code duplication check\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running jscpd code duplication detection...\"\n          npm run check:duplication\n          echo \"Code duplication check passed\"\n\n  # === FILE LINE LIMIT CHECK ===\n  # IMPORTANT: This check must validate the ACTUAL merge result, not a stale merge preview.\n  # See docs/case-studies/issue-1141 for why this is critical.\n  check-file-line-limits:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check]\n    # Run if: push event, OR changeset-check succeeded, OR changeset-check was skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: always() && (github.event_name == 'push' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') && (needs.detect-changes.outputs.mjs-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          # For PRs, fetch enough history to merge with base branch\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)\n        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'\n        env:\n          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}\n        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh\n\n      - name: Check file line limits (.mjs files and release.yml)\n        run: bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh\n\n  # === LONG-RUNNING CHECKS ===\n  # These checks take significant time (30s to 4+ minutes) and only run after all fast checks pass\n\n  # === UNIT TESTS ===\n  test-suites:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check, test-compilation, lint, check-file-line-limits]\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      !cancelled() &&\n      !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') &&\n      (github.event_name == 'push' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') &&\n      needs.test-compilation.result == 'success' &&\n      (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.check-file-line-limits.result == 'success' || needs.check-file-line-limits.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Use Node.js 20.x\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: 24.x\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm install\n\n      - name: Run test suite for solve.mjs\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for solve.mjs...\"\n          node tests/test-solve.mjs\n\n      - name: Run test suite for hive.mjs\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for hive.mjs...\"\n          node tests/test-hive.mjs\n\n      - name: Run test suite for memory-check.mjs\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for memory-check.mjs...\"\n          node tests/test-memory-check.mjs\n\n      - name: Run branch name validation test (Issue #647)\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for branch name validation...\"\n          node tests/test-branch-name-validation.mjs\n\n      - name: Run feedback lines regression test (Issue #168)\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running feedback lines regression test...\"\n          node tests/test-feedback-lines-simple.mjs\n\n      - name: Run feedback lines integration test (Issue #168)\n        env:\n          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TEST_GITHUB_USER_TOKEN || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          TEST_GITHUB_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.TEST_GITHUB_USERNAME }}\n          TEST_GITHUB_USER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TEST_GITHUB_USER_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running feedback lines integration test with real repository...\"\n          node tests/test-feedback-lines-integration.mjs\n\n      - name: Run Sentry integration test\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for Sentry integration...\"\n          node tests/test-sentry.mjs\n\n      - name: Run telegram-bot dry-run test (Issue #487)\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for telegram-bot --dry-run mode...\"\n          node tests/test-telegram-bot-dry-run.mjs\n\n      - name: Run telegram-bot hero Links Notation test (Issue #623)\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for telegram-bot hero example with Links Notation...\"\n          node tests/test-telegram-bot-hero-links-notation.mjs\n\n      - name: Run hive command silent failure test (Issue #504)\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running test suite for hive command silent failures...\"\n          node tests/test-hive-no-silent-failure.mjs\n\n      -
