The HIVE MIND. The SWARM MIND.\n\nIt is also possible to connect this AI to collective human intelligence, meaning this system can communicate with humans for requirements, expertise, feedback.\n\n[![Universal Problem Solving Algorithm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d91e911-9ba4-456e-a00a-14cdd60d9a0a)](https://github.com/konard/problem-solving)\n\nInspired by [konard/problem-solving](https://github.com/konard/problem-solving)\n\n## Why Hive Mind?\n\n**Hive Mind is the most autonomous, cloud-ready AI issue solver that eliminates developer babysitting while maintaining human oversight on critical decisions.**\n\nHive Mind is a **generalist AI** (mini-AGI) capable of working on a wide range of tasks - not just programming. Almost anything that can be done with files in a repository can be automated.\n\n| Feature                      | What It Means For You                                                                                                                                                      |\n| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| **No Babysitting**           | Full autonomous mode with sudo access. AI has creative freedom like a real programmer.                                                                                     |\n| **Cloud Isolation**          | Runs on dedicated VMs or Docker. Easy to restore if broken.                                                                                                                |\n| **Full Internet + Sudo**     | AI can install packages, fetch docs, and configure the system as needed.                                                                                                   |\n| **Pre-installed Toolchain**  | 25GB+ ready: 10 language runtimes, 2 theorem provers, build tools. Can install more.                                                                                       |\n| **Token Efficiency**         | Routine tasks automated in code, so AI tokens focus on creative problem-solving.                                                                                           |\n| **Time Freedom**             | What takes humans 2-8 hours, AI completes each working session in 10-25 minutes. Mass execution of tasks in repository is possible. \"The code is written while you sleep.\" |\n| **Scale with Orchestration** | Parallel workers feel like a team of developers, all for ~$200/month.                                                                                                      |\n| **Human Control**            | AI creates draft PRs - you decide what merges. Quality gates where they matter.                                                                                            |\n| **Any Device Programming**   | Manage AI from any device with `/solve` and `/hive` via Telegram bot. No PC, IDE, or laptop required.                                                                      |\n| **100% Open Source**         | Unlicense (public domain). Full transparency, no vendor lock-in.                                                                                                           |\n\n> _\"Compared to Codex for $200, this solution is fire.\"_ - User feedback\n\n**Cost**: Claude MAX subscription (~$200/month, currently 50% off = $400 value) provides almost unlimited usage for Hive Mind - the best value/quality balance on the market.\n\nHive Mind has high creativity indistinguishable from average programmers. It asks questions if requirements are unclear, and you can clarify on the go via PR comments.\n\nFor detailed features and comparisons, see [docs/FEATURES.md](./docs/FEATURES.md) and [docs/COMPARISON.md](./docs/COMPARISON.md).\n\n## ⚠️ WARNING\n\nIt is UNSAFE to run this software on your developer machine.\n\nIt is recommended to use Docker for installation (both locally and on servers). See the [Docker installation](#using-docker) section below.\n\nThis software uses full autonomous mode of Claude Code, that means it is free to execute any commands it sees fit.\n\nThat means it can lead to unexpected side effects.\n\nThere is also a known issue of space leakage. So you need to make sure you are able to reinstall your virtual machine to clear space and/or any damage to the virtual machine.\n\n### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Token and Sensitive Data Security\n\n**THIS SOFTWARE CANNOT GUARANTEE ANY SAFETY FOR YOUR TOKENS OR OTHER SENSITIVE DATA ON THE VIRTUAL MACHINE.**\n\nThere are infinite ways to extract tokens from a virtual machine connected to the internet. This includes but is not limited to:\n\n- **Claude MAX tokens** - Required for AI operations\n- **GitHub tokens** - Required for repository access\n- **API keys and credentials** - Any sensitive data on the system\n\n**IMPORTANT SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS:**\n\n- Running on a developer machine is **ABSOLUTELY UNSAFE**\n- Running on a virtual machine is **LESS UNSAFE** but still has risks\n- Even though your developer machine data isn't directly exposed, the VM still contains sensitive tokens\n- Any token stored on an internet-connected system can potentially be compromised\n\n**USE THIS SOFTWARE ENTIRELY AT YOUR OWN RISK AND RESPONSIBILITY.**\n\nWe strongly recommend:\n\n- Using dedicated, isolated virtual machines\n- Rotating tokens regularly\n- Monitoring token usage for suspicious activity\n- Never using production tokens or credentials\n- Being prepared to revoke and replace all tokens used with this system\n\nMinimum system requirements to run `hive.mjs`:\n\n```\n1 CPU Core\n1 GB RAM\n> 4 GB SWAP\n50 GB disk space\n```\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start\n\n### Global Installation\n\n#### Using Bun (Recommended)\n\n```bash\nbun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind\n```\n\n#### Using Node.js\n\n```bash\nnpm install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind\n```\n\n### Installing Docker\n\nIf you don't have Docker installed yet, follow these steps to install Docker Engine on Ubuntu:\n\n```bash\n# Install prerequisites\nsudo apt update\nsudo apt install ca-certificates curl\n\n# Add Docker's official GPG key\nsudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings\nsudo curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc\nsudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc\n\n# Add Docker repository\nsudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.sources <<EOF\nTypes: deb\nURIs: https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu\nSuites: $(. /etc/os-release && echo \"${UBUNTU_CODENAME:-$VERSION_CODENAME}\")\nComponents: stable\nSigned-By: /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc\nEOF\n\n# Install Docker\nsudo apt update\nsudo apt install docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin\n\n# Verify installation\nsudo docker run hello-world\n```\n\n**For other operating systems** or detailed instructions, see the [official Docker documentation](https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/).\n\n### Using Docker\n\nRun the Hive Mind using Docker for safer local installation - no manual setup required:\n\n**Note:** Docker is much safer for local installation and can be used to install multiple isolated instances on a server or Kubernetes cluster. For Kubernetes deployments, see the [Helm chart installation](#helm-installation-kubernetes-experimental) section below.\n\n```bash\n# Pull the latest image from Docker Hub\ndocker pull konard/hive-mind:latest\n\n# Start hive-mind container\ndocker run -dit --name hive-mind konard/hive-mind:latest\n\n# Verify container started\ndocker ps -a\n\n# Enter additional terminal process to do installation\ndocker exec -it hive-mind /bin/bash\n\n# Inside the container, authenticate with GitHub\ngh-setup-git-identity\n\n# Authenticate with Claude\nclaude\n\n# Optionally set configuration like this:\n# Use /config command and set:\n# Reduce motion                             true # Will save your ssh trafic, and make Claude Code more responsive (less latency)\n# Thinking mode                             false # Anthropic models perform better and cheaper without thinking\n# Model                                     haiku # chepear for connection testing manually\n# Claude in Chrome enabled by default       false # No need for Chrome support on server\n\n# Optionally test Claude connection\nclaude -p hi --model haiku\n\n# You might need to update hive-mind and agent to latest versions:\nbun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind\nbun install -g @link-assistant/agent\n\n# Now you can use hive and solve commands\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123\n\n# Or you can run telegram bot:\n\n# Exit from additional bash session\nexit\n\n# Attach to main bash process\ndocker attach hive-mind\n\n# Run bot here\n\n# Press Ctrl + P, Ctrl + Q to detach without destroying the container (no stopping of main bash process)\n\n# --- Persisting auth data across restarts ---\n\n# Extract auth data from a running (or stopped) container to the host:\nmkdir -p ~/.hive-mind\ndocker cp hive-mind:/workspace/.claude ~/.hive-mind/claude\ndocker cp hive-mind:/workspace/.claude.json ~/.hive-mind/claude.json\ndocker cp hive-mind:/workspace/.config/gh ~/.hive-mind/gh\n\n# Fix ownership to match the hive user inside the container:\nHIVE_UID=$(docker exec hive-mind id -u hive)\nchown -R $HIVE_UID:$HIVE_UID ~/.hive-mind/claude ~/.hive-mind/gh\nchown $HIVE_UID:$HIVE_UID ~/.hive-mind/claude.json\n\n# On subsequent runs, mount the auth data to keep it between restarts:\ndocker run -dit \\\n  --name hive-mind \\\n  --restart unless-stopped \\\n  -v /root/.hive-mind/claude:/home/hive/.claude \\\n  -v /root/.hive-mind/claude.json:/home/hive/.claude.json \\\n  -v /root/.hive-mind/gh:/home/hive/.config/gh \\\n  konard/hive-mind:latest\n```\n\n**Benefits of Docker:**\n\n- ✅ Pre-configured Ubuntu 24.04 environment\n- ✅ All dependencies pre-installed\n- ✅ Isolated from your host system\n- ✅ Easy to run multiple instances with different GitHub accounts\n- ✅ Consistent environment across different machines\n\nSee [docs/DOCKER.md](./docs/DOCKER.md) for advanced Docker usage.\n\n#### Stoping and removing docker container\n\n```\n# Attach to main docker process to stop the container\ndocker attach hive-mind\n\n^C # stop the telegram bot\n\nexit # exit/stop the container\n\ndocker ps -a # show list of docker containers\n# CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                     COMMAND       CREATED      STATUS                        PORTS     NAMES\n# fd0fd4470ec3   konard/hive-mind:latest   \"/bin/bash\"   5 days ago   Exited (130) 16 seconds ago             hive-mind\n\n\ndf -h # check disk space\n# Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on\n# tmpfs           1.2G  1.1M  1.2G   1% /run\n# /dev/sda1        96G   87G  9.8G  90% /\n# tmpfs           5.9G     0  5.9G   0% /dev/shm\n# tmpfs           5.0M     0  5.0M   0% /run/lock\n# /dev/sda16      881M  117M  703M  15% /boot\n# /dev/sda15      105M  6.2M   99M   6% /boot/efi\n# tmpfs           1.2G   12K  1.2G   1% /run/user/0\n\ndocker rm hive-mind # remove docker container frees space used by the container, does not delete image\n\ndf -h # check disk space (to confirm space is freed)\n# Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on\n# tmpfs           1.2G  1.1M  1.2G   1% /run\n# /dev/sda1        96G   26G   71G  27% /\n# tmpfs           5.9G     0  5.9G   0% /dev/shm\n# tmpfs           5.0M     0  5.0M   0% /run/lock\n# /dev/sda16      881M  117M  703M  15% /boot\n# /dev/sda15      105M  6.2M   99M   6% /boot/efi\n# tmpfs           1.2G   12K  1.2G   1% /run/user/0\n```\n\n### Helm Installation (Kubernetes) (Experimental)\n\n> ⚠️ **EXPERIMENTAL:** The Helm/Kubernetes installation method is experimental and may not be fully stable.\n>\n> For a more reliable installation, we recommend using [Docker](#using-docker) instead.\n>\n> See [docs/HELM.md](./docs/HELM.md) for the full Helm installation instructions and configuration options.\n\n### Installation on Ubuntu 24.04 server (Deprecated)\n\n> ⚠️ **DEPRECATED:** This installation method is no longer recommended.\n>\n> **We now recommend using Docker for all installations**, both on developer machines and servers.\n> Docker provides better isolation, easier management, and consistent environments.\n>\n> Please use the [Docker installation method](#using-docker) above.\n> For Kubernetes deployments, see the [Helm installation](#helm-installation-kubernetes-experimental) section.\n>\n> The legacy bare-metal installation instructions have been moved to [docs/UBUNTU-SERVER.md](./docs/UBUNTU-SERVER.md) for reference.\n\n### Core Operations\n\n```bash\n# Solve using maximum power\nsolve https://github.com/Veronika89-lang/index.html/issues/1 --attach-logs --verbose --model opus --think max\n\n# Solve GitHub issues automatically\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --model sonnet\n\n# Solve issue with PR to custom branch (manual fork mode)\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --base-branch develop --fork\n\n# Continue working on existing PR\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/456 --model opus\n\n# Resume from Claude session when limit is reached\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --resume session-id\n\n# Start hive orchestration (monitor and solve issues automatically)\nhive https://github.com/owner/repo --monitor-tag \"help wanted\" --concurrency 3\n\n# Monitor all issues in organization\nhive https://github.com/microsoft --all-issues --max-issues 10\n\n# Run collaborative review process\nreview --repo owner/repo --pr 456\n\n# Multiple AI reviewers for consensus\n./reviewers-hive.mjs --agents 3 --consensus-threshold 0.8\n```\n\n## 📋 Core Components\n\n| Script                                      | Purpose                       | Key Features                                                             |\n| ------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| `solve.mjs` (stable)                        | GitHub issue solver           | Auto fork, branch creation, PR generation, resume sessions, fork support |\n| `hive.mjs` (stable)                         | AI orchestration & monitoring | Multi-repo monitoring, concurrent workers, issue queue management        |\n| `review.mjs` (alpha)                        | Code review automation        | Collaborative AI reviews, automated feedback                             |\n| `reviewers-hive.mjs` (alpha / experimental) | Review team management        | Multi-agent consensus, reviewer assignment                               |\n| `telegram-bot.mjs` (stable)                 | Telegram bot interface        | Remote command execution, group chat support, diagnostic tools           |\n\n## 🔧 solve Options\n\n```bash\nsolve <issue-url> [options]\n```\n\n**Most frequently used options:**\n\n| Option          | Alias | Description                             | Default   |\n| --------------- | ----- | --------------------------------------- | --------- |\n| `--model`       | `-m`  | AI model to use (sonnet, opus, haiku)   | sonnet    |\n| `--think`       |       | Thinking level (low, medium, high, max) | -         |\n| `--base-branch` | `-b`  | Target branch for PR                    | (default) |\n\n**Other useful options:**\n\n| Option                   | Alias | Description                                      | Default |\n| ------------------------ | ----- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------- |\n| `--tool`                 |       | AI tool (claude, opencode, codex, agent)         | claude  |\n| `--verbose`              | `-v`  | Enable verbose logging                           | false   |\n| `--attach-logs`          |       | Attach logs to PR (⚠️ may expose sensitive data) | false   |\n| `--auto-init-repository` |       | Auto-initialize empty repos (creates README.md)  | false   |\n| `--help`                 | `-h`  | Show all available options                       | -       |\n\n> **📖 Full options list**: See [docs/CONFIGURATION.md](./docs/CONFIGURATION.md#solve-options) for all available options including forking, auto-continue, watch mode, and experimental features.\n\n## 🔧 hive Options\n\n```bash\nhive <github-url> [options]\n```\n\n**Most frequently used options:**\n\n| Option         | Alias | Description                             | Default |\n| -------------- | ----- | --------------------------------------- | ------- |\n| `--model`      | `-m`  | AI model to use (sonnet, opus, haiku)   | sonnet  |\n| `--think`      |       | Thinking level (low, medium, high, max) | -       |\n| `--all-issues` | `-a`  | Monitor all issues (ignore labels)      | false   |\n| `--once`       |       | Single run (don't monitor continuously) | false   |\n\n**Other useful options:**\n\n| Option                   | Alias | Description                                       | Default |\n| ------------------------ | ----- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------- |\n| `--tool`                 |       | AI tool (claude, opencode, agent)                 | claude  |\n| `--concurrency`          | `-c`  | Number of parallel workers                        | 2       |\n| `--skip-issues-with-prs` | `-s`  | Skip issues with existing PRs                     | false   |\n| `--verbose`              | `-v`  | Enable verbose logging                            | false   |\n| `--attach-logs`          |       | Attach logs to PRs (⚠️ may expose sensitive data) | false   |\n| `--help`                 | `-h`  | Show all available options                        | -       |\n\n> **📖 Full options list**: See [docs/CONFIGURATION.md](./docs/CONFIGURATION.md#hive-options) for all available options including project monitoring, YouTrack integration, and experimental features.\n\n## 🤖 Telegram Bot\n\nThe Hive Mind includes a Telegram bot interface (SwarmMindBot) for remote command execution.\n\n### 🚀 Test Drive\n\nWant to see the Hive Mind in action? Request a free demo or get faster support by messaging the developer directly on Telegram:\n\n**[Message @drakonard on Telegram](https://t.me/drakonard)**\n\n### Setup\n\n1. **Get Bot Token**\n   - Talk to [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather) on Telegram\n   - Create a new bot and get your token\n   - Add the bot to your group chat and make it an admin\n\n2. **Configure Environment**\n\n   ```bash\n   # Copy the example configuration\n   cp .env.example .env\n\n   # Edit and add your bot token\n   echo \"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=your_bot_token_here\" >> .env\n\n   # Optional: Restrict to specific chats\n   # Get chat ID using /help command, then add:\n   echo \"TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHATS=123456789,987654321\" >> .env\n   ```\n\n3. **Start the Bot**\n\n   ```bash\n   hive-telegram-bot\n   ```\n\n   **Recommended: Capture logs with tee**\n\n   When running the bot for extended periods, it's recommended to capture logs to a file using `tee`. This ensures you can review logs later even if the terminal buffer overflows:\n\n   ```bash\n   hive-telegram-bot 2>&1 | tee -a logs/bot-$(date +%Y%m%d).log\n   ```\n\n   Or create a logs directory and start with automatic log rotation:\n\n   ```bash\n   mkdir -p logs\n   hive-telegram-bot 2>&1 | tee -a \"logs/bot-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\"\n   ```\n\n### Bot Commands\n\nAll commands work in **group chats only** (not in private messages with the bot):\n\n#### `/solve` - Solve GitHub Issues\n\n```\n/solve <github-url> [options]\n\nExamples:\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --model sonnet\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --model opus --think max\n\nFree Models (with --tool agent):\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model minimax-m2.5-free\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model opencode/minimax-m2.5-free\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model gpt-5-nano\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model big-pickle\n\nFree Models via Kilo Gateway (with --tool agent):\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model kilo/glm-5-free\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model kilo/glm-4.5-air-free\n/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --tool agent --model kilo/deepseek-r1-free\n```\n\n> **📖 Free Models Guide**: See [docs/FREE_MODELS.md](./docs/FREE_MODELS.md) for comprehensive information about all free models including OpenCode Zen and Kilo Gateway providers.\n\n#### `/hive` - Run Hive Orchestration\n\n```\n/hive <github-url> [options]\n\nExamples:\n/hive https://github.com/owner/repo\n/hive https://github.com/owner/repo --all-issues --max-issues 10\n/hive https://github.com/microsoft --all-issues --concurrency 3\n```\n\n#### `/limits` - Show Usage Limits\n\n```\n/limits\n\nShows:\n- CPU usage and load average\n- RAM usage (used vs total)\n- Disk space usage\n- GitHub API rate limits\n- Claude usage limits (session and weekly)\n```\n\n#### `/help` - Get Help and Diagnostic Info\n\n```\n/help\n\nShows:\n- Chat ID (needed for TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHATS)\n- Chat type\n- Available commands\n- Usage examples\n```\n\n### Features\n\n- ✅ **Group Chat Only**: Commands work only in group chats (not private messages)\n- ✅ **Full Options Support**: All command-line options work in Telegram\n- ✅ **Screen Sessions**: Commands run in detached screen sessions\n- ✅ **Chat Restrictions**: Optional whitelist of allowed chat IDs\n- ✅ **Diagnostic Tools**: Get chat ID and configuration info\n\n### Security Notes\n\n- Only works in group chats where the bot is admin\n- Optional chat ID restrictions via `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHATS`\n- Commands run as the system user running the bot\n- Ensure proper authentication (`gh auth login`, `claude-profiles`)\n\n## 🏆 Best Practices\n\nHive Mind works even better when repositories have strong CI/CD pipelines and clear issue requirements. See:\n\n- [BEST-PRACTICES.md](./docs/BEST-PRACTICES.md) — Universal prompts, issue writing guidelines, architecture improvement, and subagent patterns\n- [CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md](./docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md) — CI/CD pipeline setup, recommended templates, and enforcement strategies\n\nKey benefits of proper CI/CD:\n\n- AI solvers iterate until all checks pass\n- Consistent quality regardless of human/AI team composition\n- File size limits ensure code is readable by both AI and humans\n\nReady-to-use templates are available for JavaScript, Rust, Python, Go, C#, and Java.\n\n## 🏗️ Architecture\n\nThe Hive Mind operates on three layers:\n\n1. **Orchestration Layer** (`hive.mjs`) - Coordinates multiple AI agents\n2. **Execution Layer** (`solve.mjs`, `review.mjs`) - Performs specific tasks\n3. **Human Interface Layer** - Enables human-AI collaboration\n\n### Data Flow\n\n#### Mode 1: Issue → Pull Request Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant H as Human\n    participant GH as GitHub\n    participant AI as AI Agent\n    participant HM as Hive Mind\n\n    H->>GH: Creates Issue\n    Note over H,GH: Primary human input\n\n    GH->>HM: Issue Available\n    HM->>AI: Assigns Issue\n    AI->>GH: Analyzes Issue\n    AI->>AI: Develops Solution\n    AI->>GH: Creates Draft PR\n\n    Note over H,GH: Human decision point\n    GH->>H: Notifies PR Created\n    H->>GH: Reviews PR\n\n    alt Approve & Merge\n        H->>GH: Merges PR\n        GH->>HM: PR Merged\n    else Request Changes\n        H->>GH: Adds Comments\n        Note over H,GH: Secondary human input\n        GH->>HM: Comments Added\n        HM->>AI: Process Feedback\n        AI->>GH: Updates PR\n    else Close PR\n        H->>GH: Closes PR\n        GH->>HM: PR Closed\n    end\n```\n\n#### Mode 2: Pull Request → Comments Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant H as Human\n    participant GH as GitHub\n    participant AI as AI Agent\n    participant HM as Hive Mind\n\n    Note over GH: Existing PR\n    H->>GH: Adds Comment\n    Note over H,GH: Primary human input\n\n    GH->>HM: New Comment Available\n    HM->>AI: Processes Comment\n    AI->>GH: Analyzes Feedback\n    AI->>AI: Updates Solution\n    AI->>GH: Pushes Changes\n\n    Note over H,GH: Human decision point\n    GH->>H: Notifies Changes\n    H->>GH: Reviews Updates\n\n    alt Approve & Merge\n        H->>GH: Merges PR\n        GH->>HM: PR Merged\n    else More Changes Needed\n        H->>GH: Adds More Comments\n        Note over H,GH: Continued human input\n        GH->>HM: Comments Added\n    else Close PR\n        H->>GH: Closes PR\n        GH->>HM: PR Closed\n    end\n```\n\n📖 **For comprehensive data flow documentation including human feedback integration points, see [docs/flow.md](./docs/flow.md)**\n\n## 📊 Usage Examples\n\n### Automated Issue Resolution\n\n```bash\n# Solve issue (automatically forks if no write access)\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --model opus\n\n# Manual fork and solve issue (works for both public and private repos)\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --fork --model opus\n\n# Continue work on existing PR\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/456 --verbose\n\n# Solve with detailed logging and solution attachment\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --verbose --attach-logs\n\n# Dry run to see what would happen\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --dry-run\n```\n\n### Multi-Repository Orchestration\n\n```bash\n# Monitor single repository with specific label\nhive https://github.com/owner/repo --monitor-tag \"bug\" --concurrency 4\n\n# Monitor all issues in an organization\nhive https://github.com/microsoft --all-issues --max-issues 20 --once\n\n# Monitor user repositories with high concurrency\nhive https://github.com/username --all-issues --concurrency 8 --interval 120\n\n# Skip issues that already have PRs\nhive https://github.com/org/repo --skip-issues-with-prs --verbose\n\n# Auto-cleanup temporary files\nhive https://github.com/org/repo --auto-cleanup --concurrency 5\n```\n\n### Session Management\n\n```bash\n# Resume when Claude hits limit\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --resume 657e6db1-6eb3-4a8d\n\n# Continue session interactively in Claude Code\n(cd /tmp/gh-issue-solver-123456789 && claude --resume session-id)\n```\n\n## 🔍 Monitoring & Logging\n\nFind resume commands in logs:\n\n```bash\ngrep -E '\\(cd /tmp/gh-issue-solver-[0-9]+ && claude --resume [0-9a-f-]{36}\\)' hive-*.log\n```\n\n## 🔧 Configuration\n\n**Authentication:**\n\n- `gh auth login` - GitHub CLI authentication\n- `claude-profiles` - Claude authentication profile migration to server\n\n**OpenRouter Integration:**\n\nUse OpenRouter to access 500+ AI models from 60+ providers with a single API key. See [docs/OPENROUTER.md](./docs/OPENROUTER.md) for setup instructions covering both Claude Code CLI and @link-assistant/agent.\n\n**Environment Variables & Advanced Options:**\n\nFor comprehensive configuration including environment variables, timeouts, retry limits, Telegram bot settings, YouTrack integration, and all CLI options, see [docs/CONFIGURATION.md](./docs/CONFIGURATION.md).\n\n## 🐛 Reporting Issues\n\n### Hive Mind Issues\n\nIf you encounter issues with **Hive Mind** (this project), please report them on our GitHub Issues page:\n\n- **Repository**: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind\n- **Issues**: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues\n\n### Claude Code CLI Issues\n\nIf you encounter issues with the **Claude Code CLI** itself (e.g., `claude` command errors, installation problems, or CLI bugs), please report them to the official Claude Code repository:\n\n- **Repository**: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code\n- **Issues**: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues\n\n## 🛡️ File Size Enforcement\n\nAll documentation files are automatically checked:\n\n```bash\nfind docs/ -name \"*.md\" -exec wc -l {} + | awk '$1 > 1000 {print \"ERROR: \" $2 \" has \" $1 \" lines (max 1000)\"}'\n```\n\n## Server diagnostics\n\nIdentify screens that are parents of processes that eating the resources\n\n```bash\nTARGETS=\"62220 65988 63094 66606 1028071 4127023\"\n\n# build screen PID -> session name map\ndeclare -A NAME\nwhile read -r id; do spid=${id%%.*}; NAME[$spid]=\"$id\"; done \\\n  < <(screen -ls | awk '/(Detached|Attached)/{print $1}')\n\n# check each PID's environment for STY and map back to session\nfor p in $TARGETS; do\n  sty=$(tr '\\0' '\\n' < /proc/$p/environ 2>/dev/null | awk -F= '$1==\"STY\"{print $2}')\n  if [ -n \"$sty\" ]; then\n    spid=${sty%%.*}\n    echo \"$p  ->  ${NAME[$spid]:-$sty}\"\n  else\n    echo \"$p  ->  (no STY; not from screen or env cleared / double-forked)\"\n  fi\ndone\n```\n\nShow details about the proccess\n\n```bash\nprocinfo() {\n  local pid=$1\n  if [ -z \"$pid\" ]; then\n    echo \"Usage: procinfo <pid>\"\n    return 1\n  fi\n  if [ ! -d \"/proc/$pid\" ]; then\n    echo \"Process $pid not found.\"\n    return 1\n  fi\n\n  echo \"=== Process $pid ===\"\n  # Basic process info\n  ps -p \"$pid\" -o user=,uid=,pid=,ppid=,c=,stime=,etime=,tty=,time=,cmd=\n\n  echo\n  # Working directory\n  echo \"CWD: $(readlink -f /proc/$pid/cwd 2>/dev/null)\"\n\n  # Executable path\n  echo \"EXE: $(readlink -f /proc/$pid/exe 2>/dev/null)\"\n\n  # Root directory of the process\n  echo \"ROOT: $(readlink -f /proc/$pid/root 2>/dev/null)\"\n\n  # Command line (full, raw)\n  echo \"CMDLINE:\"\n  tr '\\0' ' ' < /proc/$pid/cmdline 2>/dev/null\n  echo\n\n  # Environment variables\n  echo\n  echo \"ENVIRONMENT (key=value):\"\n  tr '\\0' '\\n' < /proc/$pid/environ 2>/dev/null | head -n 20\n\n  # Open files (first few)\n  echo\n  echo \"OPEN FILES:\"\n  ls -l /proc/$pid/fd 2>/dev/null | head -n 10\n\n  # Child processes\n  echo\n  echo \"CHILDREN:\"\n  ps --ppid \"$pid\" -o pid=,cmd= 2>/dev/null\n}\nprocinfo 62220\n```\n\n## Maintenance\n\n### Enter latest screen\n\n```bash\ns=$(screen -ls | awk '/Detached/ {print $1; exit}'); echo \"Entering $s\"; screen -r \"$s\"; echo \"Left $s\";\n```\n\n### Enter oldest screen\n\n```bash\ns=$(screen -ls | awk '/Detached/ {last=$1} END{print last}'); echo \"Entering $s\"; screen -r \"$s\"; echo \"Left $s\";\n```\n\n### Reboot server.\n\n```bash\nsudo reboot\n```\n\nThat will remove all dangling unused proccesses and screens, which will in turn free the RAM and reduce CPU load. Also reboot may clear all temporary files, so next step can do nothing if reboot was done.\n\n### Cleanup disk space.\n\n```bash\ndf -h\n\nrm -rf /tmp\n\ndf -h\n```\n\nThese commands should be executed under `hive` user. If you have accidentally removed the `/tmp` folder itself under `root` user, you will need to restore it like this:\n\n```bash\nsudo mkdir -p /tmp\nsudo chown root:root /tmp\nsudo chmod 1777 /tmp\n```\n\n### Close all screens to free up RAM\n\n```bash\n# close all (Attached or Detached) sessions\nscreen -ls | awk '/(Detached|Attached)/{print $1}' \\\n| while read s; do screen -S \"$s\" -X quit; done\n\n# remove any zombie sockets\nscreen -wipe\n\n# verify\nscreen -ls\n```\n\n### Top with full arguments of each command\n\n```bash\ntop -c\n```\n\n### See the full tree of processes\n\n```bash\nps -eo pid,ppid,user,args --forest\n```\n\nor\n\n```bash\nps axjf\n```\n\n### Kill all commands spawned by specific task\n\n```bash\npkill -f gh-issue-solver-1773073065743\n```\n\n### Kill all headless browsers spawned by ms-playwright\n\n```bash\npkill -f ms-playwright/chromium_headless_shell-1200\n```\n\nThat can be done, but not recommended as reboot have better effect.\n\n## 📄 License\n\nUnlicense License - see [LICENSE](./LICENSE)\n\n## 🤖 Contributing\n\nThis project uses AI-driven development. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./docs/CONTRIBUTING.md) for human-AI collaboration guidelines.\n",

(Recommended)\n\n```bash\n# Pull the latest image\ndocker pull konard/hive-mind:latest\n\n# Run an interactive session\ndocker run -it konard/hive-mind:latest\n\n# IMPORTANT: Authentication is done run -it hive-mind:local\n```\n\n### Option 3: Development Mode (Gitpod-style)\n\nFor development purposes, the legacy `Dockerfile` provides a Gitpod-compatible environment:\n\n```bash\n# Build the    hive-mind-dev\n```\n\n## Authentication\n\nThe production Docker image (`Dockerfile`) uses Ubuntu 24.04 and the official installation script. **IMPORTANT:** Authentication is performed **inside the container AFTER** the Docker image is fully installed and running.\n\n**Why Authentication Happens After Installation:**\n\n- ✅ Avoids Docker build timeouts caused by interactive prompts\n- ✅ Prevents build failures in CI/CD pipelines\n- ✅ Allows the installation script to complete successfully\n- ✅ Supports automated Docker image builds\n\n### GitHub Authentication\n\n```bash\n# Inside the container, AFTER it's running\ngh auth login -h github.com -s repo,workflow,user,read:org,gist\n```\n\n**Note:** The installation script intentionally does NOT call `gh auth login` during the build process. This is by design to support Docker builds without timeouts.\n\n### Claude Authentication\n\n```bash\n# Inside the container, AFTER it's running\nclaude\n```\n\nThis approach allows:\n\n- ✅ Multiple Docker instances with different GitHub accounts\n- ✅ Multiple Docker instances with different Claude subscriptions\n- ✅ No credential leakage between containers\n- ✅ Each container has its own isolated authentication\n- ✅ Successful Docker builds without interactive authentication\n\n## Prerequisites\n\n1. **Docker:** Install Docker Desktop or Docker Engine (version 20.10 or higher)\n2. **Internet Connection:** Required for pulling images and authentication\n\n## Directory Structure\n\n```\n.\n├── Dockerfile                    # Production image using Ubuntu 24.04\n├── experiments/\n│   └── solve-dockerize/\n│       └── Dockerfile            # Legacy Gitpod-compatible image (archived)\n├── scripts/\n│   └── ubuntu-24-server-install.sh  # Installation script used by Dockerfile\n└── docs/\n    └── DOCKER.md                 # This file\n```\n\n## Advanced Usage\n\n### Running with Persistent Storage\n\nTo persist authentication and work between container restarts:\n\n```bash\n# Create a volume for the hive user's home directory\ndocker volume create hive-home\n\n# Run with the volume mounted\ndocker run -it -v hive-home:/home/hive konard/hive-mind:latest\n```\n\n### Running in Detached Mode\n\n```bash\n# Start a detached container\ndocker run -d --name hive-worker -v hive-home:/home/hive konard/hive-mind:latest sleep infinity\n\n# Execute commands in the running container\ndocker exec -it hive-worker bash\n\n# Inside the container, run your commands\nsolve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123\n```\n\n### Using with Docker Compose\n\nCreate a `docker-compose.yml`:\n\n```yaml\nversion: '3.8'\nservices:\n  hive-mind:\n    image: konard/hive-mind:latest\n    volumes:\n      - hive-home:/home/hive\n    stdin_open: true\n    tty: true\n\nvolumes:\n  hive-home:\n```\n\nThen run:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose run --rm hive-mind\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### GitHub Authentication Issues\n\n```bash\n# Inside the container, check authentication status\ngh auth status\n\n# Re-authenticate if needed\ngh auth login -h github.com -s repo,workflow,user,read:org,gist\n```\n\n### Claude Authentication Issues\n\n```bash\n# Inside the container, re-run Claude to authenticate\nclaude\n```\n\n### Docker Issues\n\n```bash\n# Check Docker status on host\ndocker info\n\n# Pull the latest image\ndocker pull konard/hive-mind:latest\n\n# Rebuild from source\ndocker build -t hive-mind:local .\n```\n\n### Build Issues\n\nIf you encounter issues building the image locally:\n\n1. Ensure you have enough disk space (at least 20GB free)\n2. Check your internet connection\n3. Try building with more verbose output:\n   ```bash\n   docker build -t hive-mind:local --progress=plain .\n   ```\n\n## CI/CD Configuration for Docker Hub Publishing\n\nIf you're maintaining a fork or want to publish to your own Docker Hub account, follow these steps to configure GitHub Actions:\n\n### Step 1: Create a Docker Hub Account\n\n1. Go to [hub.docker.com](https://hub.docker.com)\n2. Sign up or log in to your account\n3. Note your Docker Hub username (e.g., `konard`)\n\n### Step 2: Generate a Docker Hub Access Token\n\n1. Log in to [hub.docker.com](https://hub.docker.com)\n2. Click on your username in the top-right corner\n3. Select **Account Settings** → **Security**\n4. Click **New Access Token**\n5. Enter a description (e.g., \"GitHub Actions - Hive Mind\")\n6. Set permissions to **Read, Write, Delete** (required for publishing)\n7. Click **Generate**\n8. **IMPORTANT:** Copy the token immediately - you won't be able to see it again!\n   - Example format: `dckr_pat_1a2b3c4d5e6f7g8h9i0j1k2l3m4n5o6p`\n\n### Step 3: Add Secrets to GitHub Repository\n\n1. Go to your GitHub repository (e.g., `https://github.com/konard/hive-mind`)\n2. Click **Settings** → **Secrets and variables** → **Actions**\n3. Click **New repository secret**\n4. Add the following two secrets:\n\n   **Secret 1: DOCKERHUB_USERNAME**\n   - Name: `DOCKERHUB_USERNAME`\n   - Value: Your Docker Hub username (e.g., `konard`)\n   - Click **Add secret**\n\n   **Secret 2: DOCKERHUB_TOKEN**\n   - Name: `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN`\n   - Value: The access token you generated in Step 2\n   - Click **Add secret**\n\n### Step 4: Update Docker Image Name\n\nIf using a fork, update the image name in `.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml`:\n\n```yaml\nenv:\n  REGISTRY: docker.io\n  IMAGE_NAME: YOUR_DOCKERHUB_USERNAME/hive-mind # Change this to your username\n```\n\n### Step 5: Verify the Configuration\n\n1. Push changes to the `main` branch\n2. Go to **Actions** tab in your GitHub repository\n3. Find the \"Docker Build and Publish\" workflow\n4. Check that it completes successfully\n5. Verify the image appears on [hub.docker.com/r/YOUR_USERNAME/hive-mind](https://hub.docker.com/r/konard/hive-mind)\n\n### How It Works\n\n- **On Pull Requests:** The workflow tests building the Docker image without publishing\n- **On Main Branch:** The workflow builds and publishes to Docker Hub with the `latest` tag\n- **On Version Tags:** The workflow publishes with semantic version tags (e.g., `v0.37.0`, `0.37`, `0`)\n\n### Troubleshooting CI/CD\n\n**Build fails with authentication error:**\n\n- Verify `DOCKERHUB_USERNAME` matches your Docker Hub username exactly\n- Regenerate `DOCKERHUB_TOKEN` and update the secret\n\n**Image published but can't pull:**\n\n- Ensure the repository on Docker Hub is public (or you're authenticated)\n- Check [hub.docker.com](https://hub.docker.com) → Your repositories → hive-mind → Settings → Make Public\n\n**Build succeeds but image doesn't appear:**\n\n- Check you're pushing to the `main` branch (pull requests only test, don't publish)\n- Verify the workflow ran in the Actions tab\n- Check Docker Hub rate limits haven't been exceeded\n\n## Security Notes\n\n- Each container maintains its own isolated authentication\n- No credentials are shared between containers\n- No credentials are stored in the Docker image itself\n- Authentication happens inside the container after it starts\n- Each GitHub/Claude account can have its own container instance\n- Docker Hub access tokens should be stored only as GitHub Secrets, never committed to the repository\n",

name: Test memory-check failure conditions\n        run: |\n          echo \"Testing with impossible memory requirement (should fail)...\"\n          if ./src/memory-check.mjs --min-memory 999999 --exit-on-failure --quiet --json; then\n            echo \"ERROR: Should have failed with impossible memory requirement\"\n            exit 1\n          else\n            echo \"Correctly failed with impossible memory requirement\"\n          fi\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"Testing with impossible disk requirement (should fail)...\"\n          if ./src/memory-check.mjs --min-disk-space 999999999 --exit-on-failure --quiet --json; then\n            echo \"ERROR: Should have failed with impossible disk requirement\"\n            exit 1\n          else\n            echo \"Correctly failed with impossible disk requirement\"\n          fi\n\n  # === DOCUMENTATION VALIDATION (FAST) ===\n  validate-docs:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check]\n    # Run if: push event, OR changeset-check succeeded, OR changeset-check was skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: always() && (github.event_name == 'push' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') && needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true'\n\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Use Node.js 20.x\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: 24.x\n\n      - name: Validate documentation files\n        run: |\n          echo \"Running documentation validation tests...\"\n          chmod +x tests/docs-validation.mjs\n          node tests/docs-validation.mjs\n\n  # === DOCKER PR CHECK ===\n  docker-pr-check:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check, test-compilation, lint, check-file-line-limits]\n    # Run if: changeset-check succeeded OR was skipped (docs-only PR), and required checks passed\n    if: |\n      always() &&\n      !cancelled() &&\n      !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') &&\n      github.event_name == 'pull_request' &&\n      (needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.test-compilation.result == 'success' || needs.test-compilation.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.check-file-line-limits.result == 'success' || needs.check-file-line-limits.result == 'skipped') &&\n      (needs.detect-changes.outputs.docker-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    env:\n      REGISTRY: docker.io\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Free up disk space\n        run: node scripts/free-disk-space.mjs\n\n      - name: Show sandbox base image version\n        run: |\n          SANDBOX_VERSION=$(grep '^FROM konard/sandbox:' Dockerfile | sed 's/FROM konard\\/sandbox://')\n          echo \"Sandbox base image version: konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION}\"\n          echo \"This pinned version ensures stable, reproducible builds.\"\n\n      - name: Build Docker image\n        run: |\n          SANDBOX_VERSION=$(grep '^FROM konard/sandbox:' Dockerfile | sed 's/FROM konard\\/sandbox://')\n          echo \"Building Docker image from konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION} base...\"\n          echo \"Note: General-purpose tools are inherited from pinned konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION}\"\n          echo \"This image adds AI-specific tools on top of the sandbox base.\"\n          echo \"\"\n\n          # Note: Multi-platform builds (amd64+arm64) are tested in docker-publish job during release\n          # PR checks only validate amd64 using standard docker build (not buildx) to ensure\n          # the image is properly loaded into Docker daemon for container verification\n          docker build --progress=plain -t ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:test . 2>&1 | tee build-output.log\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"Docker image built successfully\"\n          docker images | grep -E \"${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}|REPOSITORY\"\n\n      - name: Verify build completed without errors\n        run: |\n          echo \"Checking build logs for critical errors...\"\n\n          # Check for useradd failure (hive user creation in sandbox group)\n          if grep -E 'useradd: command not found|useradd:.*already exists' build-output.log; then\n            echo \"ERROR: Hive user creation failed\"\n            exit 1\n          fi\n\n          # Check for unbound variable errors (indicates shell script issues with set -u compatibility)\n          if grep -E 'unbound variable' build-output.log; then\n            echo \"ERROR: Unbound variable error detected in Docker build\"\n            echo \"This typically indicates a shell script issue with set -u compatibility\"\n            grep -E 'unbound variable' build-output.log || true\n            exit 1\n          fi\n\n          echo \"Build log check completed\"\n\n      - name: Verify system & development tools in running container\n        run: |\n          SANDBOX_VERSION=$(grep '^FROM konard/sandbox:' Dockerfile | sed 's/FROM konard\\/sandbox://')\n          echo \"=== Verifying hive-mind Docker image ===\"\n          echo \"Base: konard/sandbox:${SANDBOX_VERSION} (pinned) + AI-specific tools\"\n          echo \"\"\n\n          # Run verification script inside container (mounts script as read-only volume)\n          # Verifies: hive user setup (sandbox group, /workspace access), all dev tools, AI-specific tools\n          # See scripts/verify-docker-image.sh for the full list of checks\n          docker run --rm \\\n            -v \"$(pwd)/scripts/verify-docker-image.sh:/verify-docker-image.sh:ro\" \\\n            ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:test \\\n            bash /verify-docker-image.sh\n\n          echo \"\"\n          echo \"All system & development tools verification checks passed!\"\n\n  # === HELM PR CHECK (FAST) ===\n  helm-pr-check:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, changeset-check]\n    # Only runs when helm chart, package.json, or workflow files changed (Issue #1436)\n    if: always() && github.event_name == 'pull_request' && (needs.changeset-check.result == 'success' || needs.changeset-check.result == 'skipped') && (needs.detect-changes.outputs.helm-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Install Helm\n        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4\n        with:\n          version: v3.14.0\n\n      - name: Get package version\n        id: package-version\n        run: |\n          VERSION=$(node -p \"require('./package.json').version\")\n          echo \"version=$VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Package version: $VERSION\"\n\n      - name: Lint Helm chart\n        run: |\n          echo \"Linting Helm chart...\"\n          helm lint helm/hive-mind\n          echo \"Helm chart validation passed\"\n\n      - name: Verify Chart.yaml structure\n        run: bash scripts/verify-chart-yaml.sh\n\n  # === RELEASE - only runs on main after tests pass ===\n  release:\n    name: Release\n    needs: [detect-changes, lint, test-suites, test-execution, memory-check-linux]\n    if: always() && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push' && !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') && (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') && (needs.test-suites.result == 'success' || needs.test-suites.result == 'skipped') && (needs.test-execution.result == 'success' || needs.test-execution.result == 'skipped') && (needs.memory-check-linux.result == 'success' || needs.memory-check-linux.result == 'skipped') && (needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.docker-changed == 'true' || needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true')\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    outputs:\n      published: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published }}\n      published_version: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\n    # Permissions required for npm OIDC trusted publishing\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      pull-requests: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: '24.x'\n          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm install\n\n      - name: Update npm for OIDC trusted publishing\n        run: node scripts/setup-npm.mjs\n\n      - name: Check for changesets\n        id: check_changesets\n        run: |\n          # Count changeset files (excluding README.md and config.json)\n          CHANGESET_COUNT=$(find .changeset -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" | wc -l)\n          echo \"Found $CHANGESET_COUNT changeset file(s)\"\n          echo \"has_changesets=$([[ $CHANGESET_COUNT -gt 0 ]] && echo 'true' || echo 'false')\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"changeset_count=$CHANGESET_COUNT\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Harmonize changeset bump types\n        if: steps.check_changesets.outputs.has_changesets == 'true' && steps.check_changesets.outputs.changeset_count > 1\n        run: |\n          echo \"Multiple changesets detected, harmonizing bump types...\"\n          node scripts/merge-changesets.mjs\n\n      - name: Version packages and commit to main\n        if: steps.check_changesets.outputs.has_changesets == 'true'\n        id: version\n        run: node scripts/version-and-commit.mjs --mode changeset\n\n      - name: Publish to npm\n        # Run if version was committed OR if a previous attempt already committed (for re-runs)\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        id: publish_npm\n        run: node scripts/publish-to-npm.mjs --should-pull\n\n      - name: Set publish outputs\n        # This step explicitly sets the outputs using shell (more reliable than Node.js appendFileSync)\n        # It runs after npm publish and reads the script's outputs to pass them along\n        id: publish\n        if: steps.publish_npm.outputs.published == 'true' || steps.publish_npm.outputs.already_published == 'true'\n        run: |\n          echo \"published=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"published_version=${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Docker publish will be triggered with version: ${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/create-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n      - name: Format GitHub release notes\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/format-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\" --commit-sha \"${{ github.sha }}\"\n\n      - name: Post-publish - Upload Source Maps to Sentry\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"Uploading source maps to Sentry for published packages...\"\n          chmod +x scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs\n          node scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs\n\n  # === DOCKER PUBLISH ===\n  # Multi-platform builds using native runners (no QEMU). See: docs/case-studies/issue-982/\n  # ARM64 may be slow: actions/runner-images#11790, actions/partner-runner-images#101\n  # See also: docs/case-studies/issue-998/README.md\n  docker-publish:\n    name: Docker Publish (${{ matrix.platform }})\n    needs: [release]\n    # !cancelled() allows concurrency cancellation. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    # Timeout 60 min: arm64 can take 26+ min. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n    timeout-minutes: 60\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include:\n          - platform: linux/amd64\n            runner: ubuntu-latest\n            cache_suffix: amd64\n          - platform: linux/arm64\n            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm # Native ARM64, no QEMU. See: docs/case-studies/issue-998/\n            cache_suffix: arm64\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      REGISTRY: docker.io\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    outputs:\n      digest-amd64: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\n      digest-arm64: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Free up disk space\n        run: node scripts/free-disk-space.mjs\n\n      - name: Wait for NPM package availability\n        # Only run on first matrix job to avoid duplicate waits\n        if: matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64'\n        run: node scripts/wait-for-npm.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Build and push by digest\n        id: build\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v7\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ./Dockerfile\n          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}\n          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}\n          # Registry cache for faster parallel export. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }}\n          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }},mode=max,image-manifest=true,ignore-error=true\n          outputs: type=image,name=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }},push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true\n\n      - name: Export digest\n        run: |\n          mkdir -p /tmp/digests\n          digest=\"${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\"\n          touch \"/tmp/digests/${digest#sha256:}\"\n          echo \"Digest for ${{ matrix.platform }}: $digest\"\n\n      - name: Upload digest\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7\n        with:\n          name: digests-${{ matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64' && 'amd64' || 'arm64' }}\n          path: /tmp/digests/*\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          retention-days: 1\n\n  # Merge multi-platform manifests after all platform builds complete\n  docker-publish-merge:\n    name: Docker Publish (Merge)\n    needs: [release, docker-publish]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Download digests\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8\n        with:\n          path: /tmp/digests\n          pattern: digests-*\n          merge-multiple: true\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n            type=raw,value=latest\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Create manifest list and push\n        working-directory: /tmp/digests\n        run: |\n          echo \"Creating multi-platform manifest...\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n          echo \"Digests:\"\n          ls -la\n          docker buildx imagetools create $(jq -cr '.tags | map(\"-t \" + .) | join(\" \")' <<< \"$DOCKER_METADATA_OUTPUT_JSON\") \\\n            $(printf '${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@sha256:%s ' *)\n\n      - name: Inspect image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Inspecting multi-platform image...\"\n          docker buildx imagetools inspect ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Verify published image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Docker image published successfully\"\n          echo \"Image: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n\n  # === HELM CHART RELEASE ===\n  helm-release:\n    name: Helm Release\n    needs: [release, docker-publish-merge]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish-merge.result == 'success' && needs.release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      packages: write\n      pages: write\n      id-token: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Install Helm\n        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4\n        with:\n          version: v3.14.0\n\n      - name: Release Helm chart\n        run: node scripts/helm-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Upload chart to release\n        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2\n        with:\n          tag_name: v${{ needs.release.outputs.published_version }}\n          files: .helm-packages/*.tgz\n\n  # === MANUAL INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  instant-release:\n    name: Instant Release\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.release_mode == 'instant'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    outputs:\n      published: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published }}\n      published_version: ${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      pull-requests: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Node.js\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: '24.x'\n          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: npm install\n\n      - name: Update npm for OIDC trusted publishing\n        run: node scripts/setup-npm.mjs\n\n      - name: Version packages and commit to main\n        id: version\n        run: node scripts/version-and-commit.mjs --mode instant --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n\n      - name: Publish to npm\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        id: publish_npm\n        run: node scripts/publish-to-npm.mjs\n\n      - name: Set publish outputs\n        # This step explicitly sets the outputs using shell (more reliable than Node.js appendFileSync)\n        id: publish\n        if: steps.publish_npm.outputs.published == 'true' || steps.publish_npm.outputs.already_published == 'true'\n        run: |\n          echo \"published=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"published_version=${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          echo \"Docker publish will be triggered with version: ${{ steps.publish_npm.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/create-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n      - name: Format GitHub release notes\n        if: steps.publish.outputs.published == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: node scripts/format-github-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ steps.publish.outputs.published_version }}\" --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\" --commit-sha \"${{ github.sha }}\"\n\n  # === DOCKER PUBLISH FOR INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  # Same multi-platform setup as docker-publish above (native runners, no QEMU).\n  # See: docs/case-studies/issue-982/README.md and docs/case-studies/issue-998/README.md\n  docker-publish-instant:\n    name: Docker Publish Instant (${{ matrix.platform }})\n    needs: [instant-release]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    # Timeout 60 min: arm64 can take 26+ min. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n    timeout-minutes: 60\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include:\n          - platform: linux/amd64\n            runner: ubuntu-latest\n            cache_suffix: amd64\n          - platform: linux/arm64\n            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm # Native ARM64, no QEMU. See: docs/case-studies/issue-998/\n            cache_suffix: arm64\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      REGISTRY: docker.io\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n\n      - name: Free up disk space\n        run: node scripts/free-disk-space.mjs\n\n      - name: Wait for NPM package availability\n        # Only run on first matrix job to avoid duplicate waits\n        if: matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64'\n        run: node scripts/wait-for-npm.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Build and push by digest\n        id: build\n        uses: docker/build-push-action@v7\n        with:\n          context: .\n          file: ./Dockerfile\n          platforms: ${{ matrix.platform }}\n          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}\n          # Registry cache for faster parallel export. See: docs/case-studies/issue-1415/README.md\n          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }}\n          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:buildcache-${{ matrix.cache_suffix }},mode=max,image-manifest=true,ignore-error=true\n          outputs: type=image,name=${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }},push-by-digest=true,name-canonical=true,push=true\n\n      - name: Export digest\n        run: |\n          mkdir -p /tmp/digests\n          digest=\"${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}\"\n          touch \"/tmp/digests/${digest#sha256:}\"\n          echo \"Digest for ${{ matrix.platform }}: $digest\"\n\n      - name: Upload digest\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7\n        with:\n          name: digests-instant-${{ matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64' && 'amd64' || 'arm64' }}\n          path: /tmp/digests/*\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          retention-days: 1\n\n  # Merge multi-platform manifests after all platform builds complete (instant release)\n  docker-publish-instant-merge:\n    name: Docker Publish Instant (Merge)\n    needs: [instant-release, docker-publish-instant]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish-instant.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      packages: write\n\n    env:\n      IMAGE_NAME: konard/hive-mind\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Download digests\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8\n        with:\n          path: /tmp/digests\n          pattern: digests-instant-*\n          merge-multiple: true\n\n      - name: Set up Docker Buildx\n        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v4\n\n      - name: Log in to Docker Hub\n        uses: docker/login-action@v4\n        with:\n          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}\n          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Extract metadata (tags, labels) for Docker\n        id: meta\n        uses: docker/metadata-action@v6\n        with:\n          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\n          tags: |\n            type=raw,value=${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n            type=raw,value=latest\n          # Set version label to actual release version, not Git ref \"main\" (issue #1419)\n          labels: |\n            org.opencontainers.image.version=${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Create manifest list and push\n        working-directory: /tmp/digests\n        run: |\n          echo \"Creating multi-platform manifest...\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n          echo \"Digests:\"\n          ls -la\n          docker buildx imagetools create $(jq -cr '.tags | map(\"-t \" + .) | join(\" \")' <<< \"$DOCKER_METADATA_OUTPUT_JSON\") \\\n            $(printf '${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@sha256:%s ' *)\n\n      - name: Inspect image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Inspecting multi-platform image...\"\n          docker buildx imagetools inspect ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n\n      - name: Verify published image\n        run: |\n          echo \"Docker image published successfully\"\n          echo \"Image: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}\"\n          echo \"Tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}\"\n\n  # === HELM CHART RELEASE FOR INSTANT RELEASE ===\n  helm-release-instant:\n    name: Helm Release (Instant)\n    needs: [instant-release, docker-publish-instant-merge]\n    # Use !cancelled() instead of always() to allow concurrency cancellation.\n    # See: docs/case-studies/issue-1278/README.md for why this change was necessary.\n    if: \"!cancelled() && needs.instant-release.result == 'success' && needs.docker-publish-instant-merge.result == 'success' && needs.instant-release.outputs.published == 'true'\"\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      packages: write\n      pages: write\n      id-token: write\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Install Helm\n        uses: azure/setup-helm@v4\n        with:\n          version: v3.14.0\n\n      - name: Release Helm chart\n        run: node scripts/helm-release.mjs --release-version \"${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\"\n\n      - name: Upload chart to release\n        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2\n        with:\n          tag_name: v${{ needs.instant-release.outputs.published_version }}\n          files: .helm-packages/*.tgz\n\n  # === MANUAL CHANGESET PR ===\n  changeset-pr:\n    name: Create Changeset PR\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.release_mode == 'changeset-pr'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      pull-requests: write\n    steps:\n      - Installing via apt makes opam accessible system-wide.\nRUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y opam && apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*\n\n# --- Environment variables ---\n# Set environment variables EARLY so they're available in subsequent RUN commands\n# All paths use /workspace (shared directory from sandbox 1.5.0)\nENV HOME=/workspace\nENV NVM_DIR=\"/workspace/.nvm\"\nENV PYENV_ROOT=\"/workspace/.pyenv\"\nENV BUN_INSTALL=\"/workspace/.bun\"\nENV DENO_INSTALL=\"/workspace/.deno\"\nENV CARGO_HOME=\"/workspace/.cargo\"\nENV GOROOT=\"/workspace/.go\"\nENV GOPATH=\"/workspace/.go/path\"\nENV SDKMAN_DIR=\"/workspace/.sdkman\"\nENV PERLBREW_ROOT=\"/workspace/.perl5\"\nENV RBENV_ROOT=\"/workspace/.rbenv\"\n\nENV rocq install script)\nENV \"$NODE_VERSION_DIR/bin\" ]; then \\\n      ln -sf \"$NODE_VERSION_DIR/bin\" /workspace/.node-bin && \\\n      chown -h hive:sandbox /workspace/.node-bin; \\\n    fi && \\\n    # Ensure nvm directories are group-writable so hive user can npm install -g\n    chmod -R g+w /workspace/.nvm 2>/dev/null || true\n\nENV PATH=\"/workspace/.node-bin:${PATH}\"\n\n# Switch to hive user for package installations\nUSER hive\nWORKDIR /workspace\n\n# --- AI-specific packages installation ---\n# Install AI coding assistant CLIs\nRUN bun install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code || echo \"claude-code: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @openai/codex || echo \"codex: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @qwen-code/qwen-code || echo \"qwen-code: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @google/gemini-cli || echo \"gemini-cli: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @github/copilot || echo \"copilot: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g opencode-ai || echo \"opencode-ai: not yet published\"\n\n# Install hive-mind workflow utilities\nRUN bun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind || echo \"hive-mind: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @link-assistant/claude-profiles || echo \"claude-profiles: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g @link-assistant/agent || echo \"agent: not yet published\" && \\\n    bun install -g available via the .node-bin symlink in PATH\nRUN npm install -g @playwright/mcp@latest --no-fund --silent\n\n# Install Playwright CLI and browsers (architecture-aware)\nRUN npm install -g @playwright/test@latest --no-fund --silent && \\\n    ARCH=$(uname -m) && \\\n    if [ \"$ARCH\" = \"x86_64\" ] || [ \"$ARCH\" = \"amd64\" ]; then \\\n      playwright install chromium chrome firefox webkit msedge chromium-headless-shell; \\\n    else \\\n      playwright install chromium firefox webkit chromium-headless-shell; \\\n    fi\n\n# Install Playwright OS dependencies (requires root)\n# Note: HOME is overridden to /root to prevent root processes from creating\n# files under /workspace/.config with root ownership (see issue #1419)\nUSER root\nRUN HOME=/root npx playwright@latest install-deps 2>/dev/null || true\n\nUSER hive\n\n# Configure Playwright MCP for Claude CLI if available\nRUN if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then \\\n      claude mcp add playwright -s user -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --isolated --headless --no-sandbox --timeout-action=600000 --viewport-size 1920x1080 2>/dev/null || true; \\\n    fi\n\n# --- Application Setup ---\nWORKDIR /app\n\nUSER root\n\n# Copy application files from repo root\nCOPY --chown=hive:sandbox . .\n\n# Ensure scripts are executable\nRUN chmod +x *.mjs 2>/dev/null || true && \\\n    chmod +x src/*.mjs 2>/dev/null || true\n\n# Create necessary directories\nRUN mkdir -p /app/claude-logs /app/claude-sessions /app/output && \\\n    mkdir -p /workspace/.claude/plugins /workspace/.config && \\\n    chown -R hive:sandbox /app /workspace/.claude /workspace/.config\n\n# Create bash profile for hive user\n# Note: .node-bin is a symlink to the active Node.js version's bin directory\nRUN echo 'export PATH=\"/workspace/.node-bin:/workspace/.bun/bin:/workspace/.cargo/bin:/workspace/.elan/bin:/workspace/.opam/default/bin:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH\"' >> /workspace/.bashrc && \\\n    echo INTERVAL=300\nENV MODEL=sonnet\n\n# Health check\nHEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=30s --retries=3 \\\n    CMD pgrep -f \"hive.mjs\\|tail\" > /dev/null || exit 1\n\n# Copy and
